clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥60 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: B‑cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia or T‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: bone marrow involvement with ≥20 % lymphoblasts (de novo presentation). Prior treatment required: none. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to venetoclax. Biomarkers required: Philadelphia chromosome absent (Ph‑negative). Biomarkers excluded: Philadelphia chromosome‑positive disease or Burkittʼs leukemia/lymphoma or lymphoblastic lymphoma.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or breastfeeding; uncontrolled infectious diseases; active hepatitis B or C infection or documented HIV infection; major surgical procedure or radiation therapy within 4 weeks before study entry; symptomatic or untreated leptomeningeal disease or spinal cord compression; New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction or cerebrovascular accident within the past 6 months; left ventricular ejection fraction < 40%; history of another invasive solid tumour not in complete remission for at least 2 years (except non‑melanoma skin cancer or carcinoma in situ); current therapeutic use of warfarin; concomitant intake of strong CYP3A inhibitors or inducers, or consumption of grapefruit/seville orange/starfruit within three days of dosing; conditions causing malabsorption preventing reliable oral drug delivery; any additional severe acute or chronic medical or psychiatric disorder judged by the investigator to pose unacceptable risk or confound assessment of safety/effectiveness.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma—not otherwise specified; High‑grade B‑cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 and/or BCL6 rearrangements; High‑grade B‑cell lymphoma not otherwise specified; Primary mediastinal (thymic) large B‑cell lymphoma; Transformed lymphoma (e.g., transformed follicular lymphoma, transformed marginal zone lymphoma, follicular lymphoma grade 3). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after failure of ≥2 lines of chemotherapy that included an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody and an anthracycline, and either documented progressive disease after autologous stem cell transplant or ineligibility/unwillingness to undergo autologous stem cell transplant. Prior treatment required: Minimum two prior systemic regimens containing an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody and an anthracycline‑containing backbone; prior autologous stem cell transplant permissible but not mandatory. Prior treatment excluded: Prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy for any indication; prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant; prior bispecific T‑cell engaging antibody therapy; prior genetically modified T‑cell receptor‑engineered cellular therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of human immunodeficiency virus infection; active hepatitis B surface antigen positivity; untreated or viremic hepatitis C infection; pregnancy or breastfeeding; uncontrolled active systemic fungal, viral, or bacterial infection; pharmacologically uncontrolled seizures; preexisting autoimmune central nervous system disease (multiple sclerosis, optic neuritis, etc.) or other neurologic immune-mediated disorders; structural intracranial abnormalities interfering with assessment (midline shift on MRI, elevated cerebrospinal fluid opening pressure, CSF protein >150 mg/dL); recent whole-brain radiation therapy within three months; major cardiovascular events (myocardial infarction, coronary intervention, unstable angina, symptomatic congestive heart failure) within six months; left ventricular ejection fraction <45%; serum ALT/AST ≥5× upper limit of normal; total bilirubin ≥1.5 mg/dL (except Gilberts); creatinine clearance <45 mL/min; absolute neutrophils <1000/mm³, platelets <50,000/mm³, lymphocytes <100/mm³; ECOG performance status >1 (unless decline attributable solely to lymphoma); life expectancy less than three months independent of lymphoma; inability to provide informed consent; ongoing systemic corticosteroid use exceeding physiologic replacement (>10 mg prednisolone daily equivalents) outside allowable peri‑procedure limits; severe immunosuppression from non‑oncologic causes; prior allogeneic stem cell transplant; prior CAR‑T therapy; prior bispecific antibody therapy; prior T‑cell receptor‑engineered cellular therapy.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Active or progressing central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
Persistent adverse events greater than grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia and vitiligo).
Recent extensive radiotherapy (>30 % of marrow‑bearing skeleton) or focal palliation near measurable disease.
Major surgery (aside from catheter placements or biopsies) lacking adequate postoperative recovery.
Clinically significant underlying lung disease requiring systemic steroids within six months.
Acute or chronic pancreatitis or cirrhosis (Child‑Pugh classification higher than A).
Clinical hepatic impairment manifested by jaundice, encephalopathy, or variceal bleeding within 60 days.
History of liver transplantation.
Previous allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
Significant cardiovascular disorder (recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, New York Heart Association Class III/IV heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, serious arrhythmias, severe aortic stenosis).
Thromboembolic or cerebrovascular events occurring within three months.
Uncontrolled infectious illnesses—including active bacterial, fungal, or viral infections, untreated hepatitis B/C, or untreated HIV (with exceptions based on investigator approval and controlled parameters).
Known hypersensitivity to the investigational product or excipients.
Concurrent enrolment in another interventional clinical trial.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Prior invasive malignancy within three years, except appropriately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer, non‑muscle‑invasive bladder cancer, in‑situ cervix, breast, or prostate cancer with no activity for two years.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: > 18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukaemia. Histology allowed: acute myeloid leukaemia. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease **or** treatment‑naïve disease not eligible for standard induction chemotherapy. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to ivosidenib or prior exposure to venetoclax. Biomarkers required: IDH1 R132 mutation detected by local testing. Biomarkers excluded: NA
general_exclusion_criteria:
- History of allergic reaction or hypersensitivity to ivosidenib or venetoclax.  
- Receipt of any prior ivosidenib or venetoclax therapy.  
- Uncontrolled serious comorbidities (infection, lab abnormalities, psychiatric illnesses) posing undue risk.  
- Concurrent use of investigational antileukemic agents or systemic chemotherapy (except allowable intrathecal chemotherapy, hydroxyurea, or ≤1 dose of cytarabine up to 2 g/m² early in study).  
- Concomitant strong cytochrome P450 3A inducers (e.g., avasimibe, carbamazepine, phenytoin, rifampin, rifabutin, St. John’s Wort).  
- Active graft‑versus‑host disease requiring ongoing systemic therapy (chronic stable GVHD permissible after discussion).  
- Severe gastrointestinal or metabolic disorders impairing oral medication absorption.  
- Ongoing treatment for a separate active malignant disease.  
- Baseline corrected QT interval ≥ 450 msec (unless discussed with principal investigator).  
- Documented active hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus infection.  
- White blood cell count exceeding 25 × 10^9/L (hydroxyurea permitted to reduce counts).  
- Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or unwillingness to employ approved contraceptive measures (applicable to females of reproductive potential).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Known symptomatic brain metastases requiring >10 mg/day prednisone (or equivalent).  
Mesenchymal tumors of the uterus.  
Clinically meaningful ascites requiring therapeutic paracentesis or thoracentesis within 4 weeks of screening.  
Systemic therapy or radiation therapy administered within 3 weeks prior to first dose of study drug.  
Active, uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection, Hepatitis B infection, or Hepatitis C infection.  
History of clinically meaningful coagulopathy or bleeding diathesis.  
Cardiovascular disease including uncontrolled hypertension (>160/90 mm Hg), significant ECG abnormalities, symptomatic heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction, severe coronary artery disease (ejection fraction <20%), recent thromboembolic event, or stroke/TIA within 6 months.  
Major surgical procedure performed within 4 weeks of screening.  
Recent bowel obstruction related to current cancer occurring within the last 6 months or signs/symptoms of intestinal obstruction within 4 weeks preceding treatment initiation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma of the prostate. Cancer burden allowed: non‑metastatic disease; pathologic stage pN0 or pNx; no radiographic evidence of local, regional, or distant metastasis on PSMA PET or Axumin fluorocyclivine F18 scan. Prior treatment required: radical prostatectomy with bilateral nerve‑sparring (intracapsular or interfascial) confirmed by surgical report; plan to receive definitive proton‑bed radiation therapy. Prior treatment excluded: prior local prostate cancer therapy such as cryotherapy or brachytherapy; prior postoperative or pelvic radiation therapy; planned elective pelvic lymph node radiation therapy. Biomarkers required: serum prostate specific antigen ≥0.1 ng/mL. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Known allergy to hyaluronic acid.; Pathologic T4 disease.; Prior anorectal surgery compromising injection site (low anterior resection, abdominoperineal resection, absent rectum.).; Inflammatory bowel disease requiring steroid treatment (Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis).; Active connective tissue disorders including systemic lupus erythematosus or scleroderma.; Abnormal baseline laboratories: white blood cell count <4000/μL or >12,000/μL; hemoglobin <10 g/dL; platelets <100,000/mm³; activated partial thromboplastin time >35 seconds or international normalized ratio >1.4; alanine/aspartate transaminase >2.5× ULN; creatinine >2.0 mg/dL; bilirubin >2.0 mg/dL.; Chronic renal failure.; Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus (fasting glucose >300 mg/dL).; Acquired immune deficiency syndrome (history of AIDS); however, controlled HIV infection with CD4⁺ ≥350 cells/µL and without AIDS‐defining illnesses is permitted.; Contraindication to magnetic resonance imaging or PSMA/PET scanning (e.g., non–MRI‑compatible devices).; Presence of bilateral hip prostheses (unilateral MRI‑compatible prosthesis allowable if image quality preserved).; Unable or unwilling to comply with study procedures.; Any additional condition deemed by the investigator to preclude safe administration or completion of study therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic castration‑resistant disease with ≥1 metastatic lesion; PSMA‑positive disease. Prior treatment required: androgen receptor pathway inhibitor and taxane‑based chemotherapy and prior lutetium‑177 labelled PSMA‑directed radiopharmaceutical therapy (with documented progression on/after). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: PSMA‑positivity on PET/CT and castrate testosterone level (<50 ng/dL). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
* Receipt of any investigational agents within 28 days prior to randomization.
* receipt of any Actinium‑225 based investigational compound prior to randomization.
* History of centrally located (brain/spinal cord) metastases that are neurologically unstable, symptomatic, or requiring systemic corticosteroids for neurological stability.
* Current acute kidney injury (development within 48 hours to 7 days) or chronic kidney disease persisting ≥3 months.
* Baseline xerostomia graded ≥2 according to CTCAE version 5.
* Uncontrolled hypertension, myocardial infarction, angina pectoris, or coronary artery bypass graft surgery occurring within 6 months prior to consent, or any clinically active significant cardiovascular disease.
* Presence of another concurrent malignancy or a history of malignancy (excluding adequately treated basal cell carcinoma, actinic keratoses without recurrence for ≥3 months, or resected non‑invasive colorectal polyps) diagnosed within the preceding 5 years.
* Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status worse than 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≤30 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: diffuse anaplastic Wilms tumour. Histology allowed: diffuse anaplastic. Cancer burden allowed: stage II, III or IV disease. Prior treatment required: Surgery (upfront nephrectomy) or biopsy establishing diagnosis performed ≤2 weeks before enrollment AND, if undergoing upfront nephrectomy, sampling of ≥1 lymph node prior to enrollment. Allowed prior systemic therapy ONLY IF < 12 weeks of pre‑nephrectomy chemotherapy prescribed for presumptive favourable‐histology Wilms tumour OR < 6 weeks of postoperative chemotherapy started ≤14 days after biopsy for presumptive favourable‑histology disease (these exceptions apply solely to patients ultimately classified as having diffuse anaplastic histology). Prior treatment excluded: Systemic therapy outside the aforementioned limited windows; any additional cytotoxic chemotherapy administered beyond the permissible periods. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: ≤30 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: favourable‑history Wilms tumour at first relapse. Histology allowed: favourable. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed disease (any anatomical distribution). Prior treatment required: Achievement of complete remission after initial therapy AND frontline chemotherapy comprised EXACTLY TWO agents (primarily actinomycin D and vincristine). No chemotherapy delivered specifically for the relapse event prior to study entry. Prior treatment excluded: Receipt of >2 chemotherapy agents in the initial regimen; any chemotherapy directed toward the relapse episode before registration. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: ≤30 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: favourable‑history Wilms tumour at first relapse. Histology allowed: favourable. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed disease (any anatomical distribution). Prior treatment required: Achievement of complete remission after initial therapy AND frontline chemotherapy comprising THREE OR FOUR OR MORE agents (high‑risk: typically vincristine, actinomycin D and doxorubicin or vincristine, actinomycin D and irinotecan; very‑high‑risk: regimen M or variants containing ≥four agents). No chemotherapy delivered specifically for the relapse event prior to study entry. Additionally, baseline renal function must show creatinine clearance or isotope GFR ≥ 60 mL/min/1.73 m². Prior treatment excluded: Initial therapy involving fewer than three agents; any chemotherapy aimed at the relapse before registration. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Clonal cytopenia of unknown significance. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Absence of overt hematologic malignancy (does not meet diagnostic criteria for myelodysplastic syndrome, myeloproliferative neoplasm, chronic myelomonocytic leukemia, acute myeloid leukemia, or any other hematolymphoid malignancy) and classified as high‑risk by the Clonal Hematopoiesis Risk Calculator. Prior treatment required: detection of ≥1 pathogenic variant in any myeloid driver gene with Variant Allele Frequency ≥2 % by next‑generation sequencing of peripheral blood or bone marrow obtained within 3 months of screening. Prior treatment excluded: any anti‑cancer therapy (including cytotoxic chemotherapy, ionising radiation, immunomodulatory agents such as lenalidomide, and targeted agents such as PARP inhibitors) administered within the preceding six months or planned within the subsequent six months; concurrent primary malignancy requiring active cytotoxic chemotherapy and/or ionising radiation therapy; known inherited bone‑marrow failure disorder or germline predisposition to hematologic malignancy. Biomarkers required: pathogenic myeloid driver gene mutation with VAF ≥ 2 % and high‑risk designation on the Clonal Hematopoiesis Risk Calculator. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Concurrent primary malignancy requiring active cytotoxic chemotherapy and/or ionizing radiation therapy.
Known inherited bone marrow failure disorder and/or germline predisposition to hematologic malignancy.
Active uncontrolled systemic fungal, bacterial, or viral infection.
Recent (≤3 months) administration of a live attenuated vaccine or scheduled live attenuated vaccination within the next 15 months.
Laboratory evidence indicating clinically significant red cell hemolysis.
Hypersplenism and/or evidence of portal hypertension on examination or imaging.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18–84 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: high grade serous ovarian carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent disease restricted to abdomen or pelvis with no distant extracavitary metastases; measurable lesion per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: at least one prior systemic therapy and documentation of platinum‐resistant or intolerant disease; if a pathogenic germline or somatic BRCA1 or BRCA2 alteration is present, prior PARP inhibitor therapy as maintenance or treatment. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of distant metastasis outside the abdominopelvic cavity; recent (<3 months) bowel obstruction or high risk for obstruction; reliance on parenteral nutrition or continuous IV fluid support; simultaneous enrolment in another investigational therapeutic protocol; solid organ transplant recipient; active progression of a second malignancy or other malignancy diagnosed within 2 years (excluding adequately treated basal/SCC skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, cervical CIS, prostate IN, or other indolent tumors); severe hypersensitivity reaction to study‑related substances; irreversible immune‑related toxicity from prior checkpoint inhibition or immune‑mediated toxicity necessitating extended immunosuppression (endocrinopathies controlled on hormone replacement exempt); clinically significant autoimmune disorders (active inflammatory bowel disease, rheumatoid arthritis, systemic sclerosis, systemic lupus erythematosus, ANCA‑associated vasculitis, Guillain‑Barré syndrome, Myasthenia Gravis); systemic glucocorticoids exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents for ≥4 weeks preceding infusion; uncontrolled intercurrent medical problems—including unresolved infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmia, or unmanaged psychiatric disorder; pregnancy or lactation; HIV infection; active untreated hepatitis B or C infection; preexisting non‑infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; administration of a live attenuated vaccine within 30 days of starting study treatment; anaphylaxis to murine‑derived antibodies or iron dextran; prior grade 2 or greater hemolytic anemia.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: metastatic breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable locally advanced or distant metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: progressed on 0‑1 prior lines of metastatic systemic therapy; if hormone‑receptor positive, must have progressed on or within 12 months of completing adjuvant endocrine therapy or received ≥1 line of endocrine therapy in the metastatic setting and must have received a prior cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: any prior regimen containing a topoisomerase I‑targeting chemotherapeutic agent. Biomarkers required: HER2‑low (immunohistochemistry score 1⁺ or 2⁺ with non‑amplified in‑situ hybridization). Biomarkers excluded: HER2‑positive (IHC 3⁺ or ISH‑amplified).
general_exclusion_criteria:
Concurrent use of any other investigational or study agents intended to treat the underlying malignancy.
Receipt of a live, attenuated vaccine within 30 days prior to first study dosing.
Clinically significant corneal disease.
History of severe hypersensitivity reaction to trastuzumab deruxtecan, datopotamab deruxtecan, or any monoclonal antibody.
Major surgery within 2 weeks preceding study treatment initiation.
Uncontrolled serious intercurrent illness—including active infection, uncontrolled non‑malignant systemic disease, uncontrolled seizures, or psychiatric/social situations limiting compliance.
Past or current non‑infectious pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease requiring steroid therapy, or unresolved ILD/Pneumonitis at screening.
Significant preexisting pulmonary diseases (recent pulmonary embolism, severe asthma, COPD, restrictive lung disease, pleural effusions, autoimmune/connective‑tissue disorders involving lungs, prior total pneumonectomy).
Corrected QT interval exceeding 470 ms in females or 450 ms in males.
Cardiovascular events within six months: coronary artery bypass graft, angioplasty, vascular stenting, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, New York Heart Association class II or higher heart failure, or cerebrovascular accident/stroke.
Active secondary malignant neoplasm unless disease free ≥3 years or meets predefined low‑risk exceptions (certain in‑situ carcinomas, superficial bladder cancer, non‑melanoma skin cancer, etc.).
Known untreated or poorly controlled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.
Active or uncontrolled Hepatitis B or C viral infection.
Any active infectious process, including tuberculosis.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: renal cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: clear cell renal cell carcinoma (includes tumors with sarcomatoid differentiation). Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease with measurable lesions. Prior treatment required: at least one immune checkpoint inhibitor **and** at least one tyrosine kinase inhibitor administered in the neoadjuvant, adjuvant, or metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: prior PSMA‐targeted radionuclide therapy (e.g., ^177Lu‑PSMA‑617). Biomarkers required: presence of at least one PSMA‑avid lesion showing uptake greater than hepatic activity on a ^68Ga‑PSMA‑11 PET/CT screen (performed within 28 days before enrollment) **and** absence of any PSMA‑negative measurable lesion larger than 1 cm. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Untreated brain metastases; active leptomeningeal disease; symptomatic spinal cord compression; persistent grade >1 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia, sensory neuropathy up to grade 2 allowable); hypersensitivity reaction to substances chemically related to ^177Lu‑PSMA‑617; uncontrolled interstitial lung disease or severe resting dyspnea requiring supplemental oxygen; severe renal insufficiency (estimated glomerular filtration rate <30 mL/min/1.73 m²); active gastrointestinal diseases causing baseline grade ≥2 diarrhea (e.g., Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis); current pregnancy or breastfeeding; lack of appropriate contraception for individuals of reproductive potential; concurrent enrolment in another investigational therapeutic study; any other active malignant disease expected to interfere with safety or efficacy assessments (excluding adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, or other cancers cured ≥3 years ago).

Prior treatment excluded: Recurrent head and neck cancer, parotid gland cancer, second primary malignant neoplasm (unless treated basal cell carcinoma, cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma, or in situ cervical carcinoma); systemic immunosuppressive medication taken within four weeks of enrolment; systemic autoimmune disease affecting the salivary glands. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Positive Epstein–Barr virus capsid antibody IgM; active hepatitis C virus infection; human immunodeficiency virus infection.
general_exclusion_criteria:
- Personal or family history of acute or chronic angle‑closure glaucoma, or elevated risk thereof, including prior prophylactic treatment.
- Known allergy or hypersensitivity to glycopyrrolate.
- Current cigarette smoking or former smoking/vaping with nicotine/tobacco products within the past three years.
- Current excessive alcohol consumption or hazardous drinking pattern within the past three years (men >14 drinks/week, women >7 drinks/week in the United States; >14 units/week for both genders in the United Kingdom).
- Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus (Hemoglobin A1c greater than 7%).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: endometrial cancer. Histology allowed: any endometrial carcinoma. general_exclusion_criteria: Participants who have had chemotherapy or radiotherapy within four weeks (six weeks for nitrosoureas or mitomycin C) prior to study entry.; Adverse events from prior anticancer therapy not resolved to grade ≤ 1 (unless medically acceptable).; Receiving any other investigational agents.; Clinically uncontrolled brain metastases or meninges involvement.; Severe hypersensitivity to avelumab, M1774, or similar biologics.; Active and/or uncontrolled infections (except approved HIV/HBV/HCV cases meeting strict criteria).; Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (NYHA class III or higher heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, serious arrhythmias, recent MI, stroke, TIA, or major vascular event within 180 days).; Significant hepatic impairment exceeding protocol limits.; Renal insufficiency with creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min.; Autoimmune diseases needing systemic immunosuppression (excluding well‑managed thyroid disorders, Type I diabetes, vitiligo, psoriasis).; Prior solid-organ or hematopoietic stem‑cell transplant.; Recent severe gastrointestinal pathology (bowel obstruction, uncontrolled diarrhoea, active inflammatory bowel disease).; Psychiatric illness or social situation judged unsuitable by investigators.; Pregnancy or lactation.; QTc interval > 470 ms uncorrectable.; Concurrent use of potent CYP3A4/CYP1A2 modulators, PPIs, or prohibited meds within seven days.; Use of herbal/folk remedies (St. John’s wort, kava, ephedra, Ginkgo biloba, DHEA, yohimbé, saw palmetto, ginseng, etc.).;.Vaccinations within four weeks before starting treatment (aside from inactive vaccines).; Any additional severe acute/chronic condition posing unacceptable risk according to investigator discretion.

general_exclusion_criteria: Women who are pregnant or lactating.; Prior anti‑cancer biologic agent (including checkpoint inhibitors) within 4 weeks of day 1 (time‑specific washouts ignored for timing but complete prior biologic exposure excluded where applicable).; Active second malignant disease (unless fully treated and disease‑free ≥3 years).; Known active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (stable treated brain metastases allowed under strict conditions).; Active cardiac disease: myocardial infarction or unstable angina within 6 months, serious ventricular arrhythmia, NYHA class III or higher heart failure, left ventricle EF < 40%.; Active chronic inflammatory bowel disease or gastrointestinal perforation within 6 months.; Active serious infection requiring antibiotics.; Concurrent medical or psychiatric condition judged to compromise study conduct.; Requirement for high‑dose glucocorticoids (>10 mg prednisolone daily) or other systemic immunosuppressants.; History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organising pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, or radiographic evidence of active pneumonitis.; Participation in another investigational therapeutic trial concurrently.; Allergic reaction to any component of sacituzumab govitecan, enfortumab vedotin, pembrolizumab or their excipients.; Uncontrolled diabetes (HbA1c > 8%).; Uncontrolled tumour‑related bone pain or imminent spinal cord compression.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: male and female. Cancer type allowed: inflammatory breast cancer. Histology allowed: all invasive breast cancer histologic subtypes. Cancer burden allowed: clinical stage III inflammatory breast cancer (diagnosed within past 6 months). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic anti‑cancer therapy (chemotherapy, hormonal therapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy) for the current diagnosis, any prior radiotherapy, any prior definitive surgery for the current diagnosis. Biomarkers required: HER2‑positive status per ASCO/CAP guidelines **or** HER2‑low expression (IHC 2+/ISH–, IHC 1+/ISH–, or IHC 1+/ISH untested) **and** estrogen receptor and progesterone receptor status must be evaluable (any level). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, or unresolved/current ILD/pneumonitis at screening. Significant pulmonary comorbidity (recent pulmonary embolism, severe asthma, COPD, restrictive lung disease, pleural effusion, prior pneumonectomy). Baseline corrected QT interval >470 ms or any condition increasing risk of QT prolongation/torsade de pointe. Recent major cardiac events or procedures (coronary artery bypass graft, angioplasty, stenting, MI, unstable angina, NYHA class ≥II heart failure, stroke) within 6 months. Left ventricular ejection fraction <50%. Ongoing uncontrolled intercurrent illness (active infection, uncontrolled hypertension, uncontrolled diabetes, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias, severe GI disease causing diarrhoea, psychiatric illness limiting compliance). Chronic systemic glucocorticoid (>10 mg prednisolone/day) or other immunosuppressive therapy within the preceding 2 years. Autoimmune or inflammatory disorders deemed unsuitable (except selected mild conditions per protocol). Current pregnancy, lactation, or unwillingness to use adequate contraception; females of child‑bearing potential not using approved contraception. Positive serology for HIV, active hepatitis B (HBsAg+) or untreated hepatitis C (detectable HCV RNA). Live vaccination within 30 days prior to dosing. Concurrent strong CYP3A4, CYP2C9, or CYP2D6 modulators prohibited shortly before treatment. History of venous thromboembolism within 3 months. History of leptomeningeal carcinomatosis. Any condition, therapy, or lab abnormality judged by investigators to potentially confound study outcomes or jeopardize patient safety.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18-59 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed, untreated disease classified as high‑risk (adverse) according to European LeukemiaNet 2017 criteria. Prior treatment required: None. Prior treatment excluded: any AML‑directed therapy apart from hydroxyurea or transient all‑trans retinoic acid used before ruling out acute promyelocytic leukemia; prior anthracycline cumulative dose greater than 200 mg/m² daunorubicin or equivalent; prior hypomethylating-agent exposure when given for AML diagnosis. Biomarkers required: high‑risk/adverse genomic/cytogenetic profile per ELN 2017 (screened at enrollment) **and** FLT3 wild‑type (no ITD or TKD mutation) screened at enrollment **and** absence of t(9;22) translocation screened at enrollment. Biomarkers excluded: FLT3 mutation (ITD or TKD) **or** t(9;22) translocation.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnancy or lactation.
Performance status Zubrod ≥ 3.
Left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%.
Creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min.
Aspartate aminotransferase or alanine aminotransferase > 3 times upper limit of normal.
Total bilirubin > 2 times upper limit of normal (unless history of Gilbert’s disease).
History of Wilson’s disease or other copper‑metabolism disorder.
Uncontrolled HIV infection (detectable viral load) or ineffective antiviral therapy.
Active hepatitis B infection without suppression or detectable viral load; unresolved hepatitis C infection.
Concurrent malignancy requiring active anticancer therapy (hormonal therapy permitted).

general_exclusion_criteria: History of autologous or allogeneic haematopoietic stem cell transplant within 60 days of first dose, or receipt of systemic immunosuppressive therapy for graft‑versus‑host disease, or active clinically significant graft‑vs‑host disease. Presence of another active malignancy within the past twelve months (exception made for selected hormone‑responsive breast or prostate cancer, adequately treated in‑situ cervical carcinoma, basal cell carcinoma, locally excised cutaneous SCC, or fully resected solid tumours with anticipated survivals exceeding two years). Clinically significant uncontrolled co‑morbid illness involving renal, neurologic, psychiatric, respiratory, endocrine, metabolic, immunologic, cardiovascular or hepatic systems judged to jeopardise safe participation. Baseline corrected QT interval (QTcF) ≥480 ms, New York Heart Association functional class II or worse, or other substantial electrocardiographic or echocardiographic abnormalities. History of congenital long QT syndrome, torsades de pointes, uncontrolled ventricular/tachyarrhythmias, congestive heart failure, recent myocardial infarction, cerebrovascular accident or intracranian haemorrhage within six months. Evidence of untreated active systemic infection (bacterial, fungal or viral). Confirmed uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection or active hepatitis B (HBsAg positive or HBsAb positive with detectable HBV DNA) or hepatitis C (positive antibodies with detectable HCV RNA). Persistent febrile episode >38.5 °C within seven days absent a clearly identified source compatible with the malignancy. Pregnancy or lactation. Inability to safely swallow oral medication owing to dysphagia, gastro‑intestinal obstruction, malabsorption, short bowel syndrome, gastroparesis or comparable conditions. Established active central nervous system involvement by the malignancy. Known hypersensitivity to any constituent of LP‑118. Concomitant use of medicines recognized to markedly lengthen the QT interval and predispose to torsades de pointes.

general_exclusion_criteria: History or active autoimmune disease or immune deficiency; prior idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or current pneumonitis evident on screening CT; active tuberculosis; serious infection within four weeks; receipt of therapeutic oral or intravenous antibiotics within two weeks (except prophylaxis); clinically significant cardiovascular disease; administration of a live attenuated vaccine within four weeks or anticipated need during study; prior allogenic hematopoietic stem cell or solid organ transplantation; recent treatment with systemic immunostimulatory agents (interferons, interleukin‑2) within four weeks or five drug half‑lives; recent systemic immunosuppressive drugs within two weeks (with exceptions noted); unresolved central nervous system involvement—including untreated brain lesions, uncontrolled seizures, recent (< six months) stroke, transient ischemic attack, or subarachnoid haemorrhage.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Mucosal melanoma. Histology allowed: Malignant melanoma. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Active or uncontrolled heart failure (New York Heart Association Class III–IV) or recent (<6 months) acute coronary event, CABG, PCI, or stent placement.
Significant uncontrolled hypertension (systolic > 140 mm Hg or diastolic > 90 mm Hg despite therapy).
Clinically significant arrhythmias, prolonged QTc > 500 ms, or recent cerebrovascular accident/TIA (<3 months).
Severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh B or C) or active cirrhosis with complications.
Renal insufficiency with CrCl < 30 ml/min/1.73 m².
Untreated/uncontrolled central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
Recent (≤28 days) active infectious processes requiring antimicrobial therapy, including SARS‑CoV‑2 infection.
Ongoing systemic steroid therapy exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalent or other immunosuppression.
Active autoimmune diseases requiring systemic therapy.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

Receipt of a live or replication‑competent vaccine within thirty days of starting study medication. Pregnancy or lactation; unwillingness or inability to comply with effective contraception guidelines.

Active systemic autoimmune diseases.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years (no upper limit). Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Prior treatment required: 1–3 prior systemic therapeutic regimens for metastatic breast cancer, INCLUDING at least one regimen containing an antibody‑drug conjugate; no documented disease progression on a platinum‑based regimen administered in the metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: Prior WEE1 kinase inhibitor; disease progression on prior platinum chemotherapy in the metastatic setting; disease progression on prior pembrolizumab combined with carboplatin regimen. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor negative (≤10% nuclear stain by IHC) AND prog­estero­n receptor negative (≤10% nuclear stain by IHC) AND HER2 negative (IHC 0 or 1+ or ISH ratio < 2.0). general_exclusion_criteria: Major surgical procedure within 28 days preceding study start.; Presence of untreated, symptomatic, or actively progressing central nervous system metastases.; Current grade ≥2 peripheral neuropathy or other grade ≥2 toxicity attributable to prior therapy (aside from alopecia or anorexia).; History of serious allergy or hypersensitivity reaction to components of azenosertib, carboplatin, or pembrolizumab (grade ≥3).; Active or historical non‑infectious pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease requiring steroids.; Requirement for enteral feeding via PEG tube or dependence on total parental nutrition.; Participation in another investigational drug/device study within 4 weeks of initiating study treatment.; Receiving live attenuated vaccination within 30 days of first dose.; Uncontrolled comorbid conditions—including uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, NYHA class III/IV heart failure, recent myocardial infarction (within 6 months), uncontrolled arrhythmias, poorly controlled diabetes, active peptic ulcer disease, clinically significant hepatic impairment, renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min), or severe malnutrition.; Baseline prolonged QTc (>470 ms) or personal/family history of torsades de pointes or Long QT Syndrome, or concomitant use of medicines known to markedly lengthen QT interval.; Ongoing systemic glucocorticoid therapy exceeding 10 mg/day prednisolone-equivalent or other potent immunosuppression within 7 days of study start.; Active infectious processes including tuberculosis requiring systemic therapy.; Autoimmune disease necessitating systemic treatment within the past 2 years (apart from physiological hormonal/replacement therapy).; Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant/bear a child during study period and for stipulated postpartum/conception window.; Recent allogeneic solid-organ or stem‑cell transplantation.; Significant psychiatric disorder or substance misuse impairing compliance with study protocols.

Imminent or established symptomatic spinal cord compression.
Active central nervous system metastases or unresected/elevated intracranial disease not stabilized ≥ 4 weeks post‑radiation or surgery.
Recent (< 6 months) myocardial infarction, unstable angina, NYHA Class III–IV heart failure, or uncontrolled arrhythmia/QTcF ≥ 470 ms despite correction attempts.
Uncontrolled arterial hypertension (SBP > 160 mmHg or DBP > 100 mmHg).
Severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh C) or total bilirubin > 1.5× upper limit of normal (except up to 3× ULN in Gilberts syndrome).
Significant renal impairment (creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min).
Concurrent moderate/high potency CYP3A inducer usage.
Known hypersensitivity to valemetostat tosylate or formulation components.
Active hepatitis B (detectable HBV DNA off suppression), active hepatitis C (untreated/unresponsive), or HIV infection not controlled (undetectable VL) within past six months.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding (not applicable to male population but retained as protocol blanket criterion).

Current enrollment in another interventional clinical trial.
Treatment with other investigational anticancer agents or devices.
Use of carmustine wafer implants or proteasome inhibitors at recurrence.
Major surgery (other than surgery for recurrent diffuse glioma) within 4 weeks prior to treatment.
Standard external beam radiotherapy within 12 weeks prior to documentation of progression unless progression occurs outside the irradiated volume or is pathologically proved.
Radiotherapy exceeding 65 Gy, stereotactic radiosurgery, or brachytherapy unless recurrence is histologically confirmed.
Full‑dose anticoagulation unless coagulation parameters are therapeutically controlled and stable for ≥2 weeks.
Concomitant medication that are strong CYP2D6/CYP2C19 substrates unable to substitute.
Enzyme‑inducing antiseizure medicines (patients must switch to non‑enzyme inducing alternatives ≥2 weeks before enrollment).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Known hypersensitivity to NMS‑03305293 or temozolomide formulation constituents.
Active bacterial, fungal, viral (including HIV) infections needing systemic therapy.
QTc ≥ 460 ms (women) or ≥ 450 ms (men) or any risk factor for torsades de pointe.
Uncontrolled heart failure, electrolyte abnormalities affecting repolarization, or personal/family history of congenital Long QT Syndrome.
Clinically significant gastrointestinal diseases impairing oral drug absorption.
Recent serious cardiovascular events (myocardial infarction, unstable angina, CABG/PTCA, symptomatic CHF, stroke/TIA) within 6 months.
History of another malignant neoplasm (except basal/squamous cell skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or treated/localized cancer) unless disease free >3 years.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Active central nervous system involvement by B‑ALL (blasts detected in cerebrospinal fluid).  
Isolated extramedullary disease relapse.  
Testicular leukemia.  
History or presence of significant neurologic conditions including epilepsy, seizures, paresis, aphasia, stroke, severe traumatic brain injury, dementia, Parkinson’s disease, cerebellar disorder, organic brain syndrome, psychosis, or prior grade 4 neurotoxicity associated with chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell or bispecific antibody therapy.  
History of another malignancy (exceptions applied).  
Unresolved adverse events graded ≥2 attributed to preceding therapies.  
Recent graft‑versus‑host disease necessitating immunosuppressive medication within three weeks before initiation of AZD0486.

Renal insufficiency with estimated creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min.
History of major gastric or small‑bowel resections, inflammatory bowel diseases (Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis) or baseline grade ≥ 2 diarrhoea expected to compromise study assessments.
Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraception compliance.
Prolonged corrected QT interval (QTcF ≥ 450 ms).
Use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 inhibitors or inducers, or contraindicated CYP1A2 substrate medicines.
Concurrent use of factor Xa or thrombin (factor IIa) inhibitors (except low‑molecular‑weight heparin permissible).
Radiation exposing greater than 25 percent of bone marrow volume.
Previous bone‑targeted radionuclide therapy administered ≤ 6 weeks before study drug initiation.
Myocardial infarction or unstable angina occurring within six months preceding enrolment.
Significant gastrointestinal functional impairments affecting drug absorption.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Congestive heart failure New York Heart Association Class III‑IV.
Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min) or dialysis dependence.

Uncontrolled central nervous system metastases.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection not adequately controlled on antiretroviral therapy.
Active hepatitis B infection without adequate antiviral control.
Active hepatitis C infection with detectable viral load.
Performance status worse than ECOG 1.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Presence of a locoregional tumor amenable to definitive surgical resection or cure-intent radiotherapy/chemotherapy.  
* History of another malignant neoplasm except adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer or cervical carcinoma in situ.  
* History of lymphohistiocytic or lymphoid hyperplasia; haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.  
* Failure to return laboratory values/toxicities to grade ≤ 1 after recovery from preceding anticancer therapy.  
* Significant cardiovascular events occurring within six months prior to enrollment.  
* Active central nervous system metastasis or leptomeningeal disease.  
* Current interstitial lung disease requiring therapeutic intervention.  
* Past or current severe ophthalmologic diseases (uveitis, retinitis, corneal pathology).  
* Recent major surgery (within 30 days) or minor procedure (within 10 days) impairing wound healing.  
* Positive tests for Human Immunodeficiency Virus, active Hepatitis B or C infections.  
* Previous participation in a clinical investigation involving a selective or nonspecific CDK2 inhibitor or degraders.  
* Moderate-to-severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh Class B/C) or other serious liver disease.  
* Receipt of palliative radiation therapy within fourteen days or any form of radiation within four weeks before day 1 of study medication.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: greater than 1 year and less than 25 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: B‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: B‑cell lineage blasts. Cancer burden allowed: white blood cell count ≥50,000/µL for age 1‑to‑9.99 years **or** any white blood cell count for age 10‑to‑24.99 years **or** white blood cell count <50,000/µL combined with testicular leukemia **or** central nervous system leukemia (CNS3) **or** documented steroid pretreatment; additionally ≥25% blasts in bone marrow (or equivalent diagnostic evidence) qualifying as high‑risk disease. Prior treatment required: enrollment on APEC14B1 confirming diagnosis; allowance of corticosteroid pretreatment and/or intrathecal cytarabine before systemic therapy. Prior treatment excluded: any prior cytotoxic chemotherapy for the current diagnosis (excluding the aforementioned steroids and intrathecal cytarabine); hydroxyurea exposure >72 hours within the 7 days before initiating protocol therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients with Down syndrome are excluded. Known Charcot‑Marie‑Tooth disease excludes participation. Presence of a MYC translocation associated with mature (Burkitt) B‑cell ALL leads to exclusion. Requirement for radiotherapy at diagnosis disqualifies the patient. Pregnant female participants are excluded. Breastfeeding women planning to nurse during study or for 2 months after the last dose are excluded. Participants of reproductive potential unwilling or unable to use effective contraception throughout the study are excluded.

Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: Secretory multiple myeloma. general_exclusion_criteria:
• Autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplantation performed within 12 weeks prior to first dose, or allogenic transplantation within 1 year prior to first dose.
• Any unresolved adverse event graded ≥2 attributable to prior anticancer therapy.
• Diagnosis of non‑secretory multiple myeloma.
• Presence of active plasma cell leukemia (≥20 % circulating plasma cells or >2.0×10⁹/L).
• History of Waldenström’s macroglobulinemia.
• Light chain amyloidosis.
• POEMS syndrome.
• Major surgical procedure within 4 weeks preceding first dose or scheduled thereafter.
• Acute infectious episode requiring antimicrobial therapy occurring within 14 days before first dose.
• Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus or hypertension within 14 days before first dose.
• Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥3, or grade ≥2 accompanied by pain within 2 weeks before first dose.

general_exclusion_criteria: Men with hormone‑dependent cancers (e.g., breast, prostate, androgen‑sensitive carcinomas). Presence of intracranial neoplasm (primary brain cancer). Elevated baseline hematocrit exceeding 48%. Prostatic specific antigen concentration higher than race‑specific reference values (PSA >4 ng/mL in caucasian males, >3 ng/mL in african‑american males). First‑degree family history of prostate cancer. Clinically significant cardiovascular conditions including uncontrolled congestive heart failure, myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndromes, coronary revascularisation procedures, or cerebrovascular accident occurring within three months before enrollment. Severe untreated obstructive sleep apnoea. Cognitive impairment evidenced by mini‑mental state examination score less than twenty‑four. Renal insufficiency indicated by serum creatinine greater than 2.5 mg/dL. Hepatic injury reflected by alanine aminotransferase greater than three times the institutional upper limit of normal. Metabolic derangements comprising haemoglobin A1C over 8.5 % or body mass index greater than forty‑five kilograms per square metre. Active psychiatric disorders such as untreated unipolar depressive illness, bipolar disorder, or schizophrenia.

general_exclusion_criteria: history of interstitial lung disease or symptomatic pneumonitis; uncontrolled asthma; current symptomatic congestive heart failure (NYHA class III–IV) or left ventricular ejection fraction below institution’s lower limit of normal; unstable cardiac arrhythmias requiring therapy; unstable angina; uncontrolled hypertension (systolic > 160 mm Hg or diastolic > 100 mm Hg); myocardial infarction, coronary or peripheral arterial bypass graft, cerebrovascular accident or transient ischemic attack within 90 days before first dose; neuromuscular disorders associated with elevated creatine kinase; acute or chronic pancreatitis; history or current evidence of retinal vein occlusion or major retinal vascular disease; requirement for long‑term oral vitamin K antagonist anticoagulation (e.g., warfarin); persisting toxicity greater than grade 1 (excluding alopecia and sensory neuropathy ≤ grade 2); severe (Grade ≥ 3) hypersensitivity to monoclonal antibodies or history of anaphylaxis; vaccination with live agents within 28 days of first study drug dose (inactive vaccines permissible); pregnancy or lactation; current substance/alcohol dependence; imprisonment or involuntary confinement; serious psychiatric, cognitive, or psychosocial conditions deemed by the investigator to substantially increase risk or impede informed consent or adherence to study procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: no distant metastasis; pathologic stage pT1–pT3, pN0 (node‑negative). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or biological therapy for the current breast cancer; endocrine therapy longer than six weeks for the current cancer; hormonally based contraceptives at enrollment. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor positive (≥1% nuclear stain) **and** progesterone receptor positive (≥1% nuclear stain) **and** human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative **and** Oncotype DX recurrence score 21–25 (**OR**, if recurrence score 16–20 **then** high clinical risk defined as low grade & tumor > 3 cm **or** intermediate grade & tumor > 2 cm **or** high grade & tumor > 1 cm). Biomarkers excluded: Mammoprint high‑risk / MP2 result.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Definite clinical or radiographic evidence of metastatic disease  
• Tumors staged pT4, including inflammatory breast cancer  
• History of prior ipsilateral or contralateral invasive breast cancer (DCIS or LCIS permitted)  
• Life expectancy less than ten years owing to comorbidities  
• Baseline laboratories indicating absolute neutrophil count < 1200/mm³, platelet count < 100,000/mm³, hemoglobin < 10 g/dL, total bilirubin exceeding ULN (or >1.5×ULN with benign hyperbilirubinemia), ALT/AST > 3 times ULN, glomerular filtration rate < 30 ml/min/1.73 m²  
• Cardiac disease placing the patient at NYHA functional classification poorer than class IIb, or prior exposure to cardiotoxic agents requiring restriction  
• Non‑epithelial breast malignancies (sarcoma, lymphoma, etc.)  
• Receipt of any radiation therapy, cytotoxic chemotherapy, or targeted biologic therapy for the index breast cancer before enrolment (endocrine therapy limited to ≤6 weeks)  
• Current use of hormonal contraceptives, including progestin‑containing intrauterine devices  
• Active chronic hepatitis B infection with detectable viral load despite therapy, or unresolved hepatitis C infection with detectable viral load  
• Pregnant or breastfeeding at baseline (pregnancy test required within 2 weeks preceding entry)  
• Any condition judged by the investigators to impede protocol compliance or data integrity.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non squamous. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease with measurable lesions according to RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic therapy for advanced or metastatic non small cell lung cancer; any prior topoisomerase I‑containing chemotherapy; any prior trop2‑targeted therapy; any prior anti‑programmed death‑1, anti‑programmed death‑ligand‑1, or anti‑programmed death‑ ligand‑2 therapy; any prior other immune‑checkpoint/modulatory therapy such as cytotoxic t lymphocyte associated antigen‑4 antagonist, ox40 agonist, cd137 agonist. Biomarkers required: programmed death‑ligand 1 tumour proportion score <50% (assessed centrally by 22C3 pharmDX). Biomarkers excluded: presence of actionable genomic alterations (e.g., epidermal growth factor receptor activating mutation, anaplastic lymphoma kinase rearrangement, ros1 rearrangement, ret rearrangement, ntrk fusion, met exon 14 splice variant, braf V600E mutation, kras G12C mutation).
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis or ongoing pulmonary toxicity; uncontrolled or symptomatic cardiovascular disease including QTcF >470 ms, myocardial infarction within 6 months, serious arrhythmia, uncontrolled angina, left ventricular ejection fraction <50% or New York Heart Association class II‑IV heart failure not stabilized to class I with LVEF ≥50%, uncontrolled hypertension (>180/>110 mm Hg); severe concurrent pulmonary conditions such as pulmonary embolism within 3 months, severe asthma, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, restrictive lung disease, extensive pleural effusion, or prior total pneumonectomy; spinal cord compression; clinically active untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; recently treated brain metastases not stable for at least 2 weeks off steroids; receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days preceding first study dosing; current infection with human immunodeficiency virus, hepatitis B or C; inadequate organ function (renal, hepatic) beyond protocol limits; Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status greater than 1.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. general_exclusion_criteria: Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥2.; Active or past autoimmune disease (exceptions: vitiligo, alopecia, stable hypothyroidism, chronic skin conditions not needing systemic therapy, remote inactive disease, dietary‐treated celiac disease).; Use of systemic corticosteroids >10 mg/day prednisone equivalent or other immunosuppressants within 14 days of dosing.; Prior malignant tumors (other than non‑melanoma skin cancer, in‑situ urothelial, gastric, breast, colorectal, or cervical dysplasias) unless in complete remission ≥2 years and off therapy.; History of solid organ or allogeneic stem‑cell transplantation.; Current pregnancy or nursing.; Uncontrolled concurrent illnesses—including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmia, interstitial lung disease, severe chronic gastrointestinal disorder with persistent diarrhea, or psychiatric/socioeconomic factors impairing adherence.; Baseline ECG showing QTcF ≥470 milliseconds.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: any. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. general_exclusion_criteria:
Active infection with SARS‑CoV‑2 coronavirus, untreated HIV infection with detectable viremia, or active hepatitis B or C infection (except vaccinated HBV individuals or controlled HIV with CD4⁺ ≥ 350 cells/mm³ and suppressed viral load).
Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses including symptomatic congestive heart failure NYHA class II or greater, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias, moderate-to-severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease causing dyspnoea at rest or light activity, unresolved acute infections, or major psychiatric disorders compromising adherence.
Clinically significant ascites or pleural effusions.
Known hypersensitivity to paclitaxel.
Peripheral sensory neuropathy grade 2 or higher.
Corrected QT interval exceeding 480 milliseconds or presence of risk factors predisposing to torsades de pointe (family history of congenital long QT syndrome, electrolyte abnormalities, or concomitant medication that markedly prolongs QT/QTc despite allowance elsewhere).
Inadequate bone marrow reserve (absolute neutrophils <1500/mm³, platelets <100 × 109/L, haemoglobin <9 g/dL), impaired hepatic function (AST/ALT >3 times upper limit of normal, bilirubin >1.5 × upper limit), or compromised renal function (creatinine >1.5 × uln or estimated glomerular filtration rate ≤50 mL/min).

Prior treatment excluded: Prior exposure to cytotoxic chemotherapy, brutonʼs tyrosine kinase inhibitors, or other waldenström macroglobulinemia‑specific systemic therapies (exceptions: corticosteroids, intravenous immune globulin, anti‑CD20 antibodies). Biomarkers required: Presence of an IgM monoclonal paraprotein detected by serum immunofixation; serum IgM concentration <4000 mg/dL. Biomarkers excluded: Serum IgM ≥4000 mg/dL; diagnosis of marginal zone lymphoma, chronic lymphocytic leukemia, mantle cell lymphoma, IgM myeloma, or AL amyloidosis; uncontrolled autoimmune hemolytic anemia or immune thrombocytopenia; central nervous system lymphoma; neuropathy caused by etiologies other than IgM‑mediated mechanisms; other indications for initiating waldenström macroglobulinemia therapy besides symptomatic neuropathy.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of serious cardiovascular disease (unstable angina, symptomatic arrhythmias, myocardial infarction within 6 months, congestive heart failure NYHA Class III–IV, uncontrolled hypertension).
Active or untreated infection, including clinically significant bacterial, viral, or fungal infections at baseline.
Recent major surgical procedure (within 4 weeks preceding first dose).
Use of potent cytochrome P450 3A modulators (strong/moderate inhibitors or inducers) within 7 days of dosing.
Concurrent oral anticoagulation with warfarin or equivalent vitamin K antagonist.
Presence of gastrointestinal ulcer identified by endoscopy within 3 months.
Chronic liver disease classified as Child‑Pugh C.
Significant bleeding tendency or coagulation disorders (von Willebrand disease, hemophilia, active bleeding).
Uncontrolled pulmonary pathology (history of pneumonitis, interstitial lung disease).
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to conceive/father a child within stipulated post‑treatment intervals.
Inadequate organ/marrow function exceeding predefined limits (ANC <1000/µL, platelets <50,000/µL when cause unrelated to disease, hemoglobin <7 g/dL, total bilirubin >1.5×ULN, ALT/AST >2.5×ULN, estimated glomerular filtration rate <30 ml/min).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: younger than 50 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: bone cancer. Histology allowed: osteosarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: up to four pulmonary nodules per lung, each measuring ≥ 3 mm and ≤ 3 cm, all judged resectable by wedge excision or comparable technique without necessitating lobectomy or pneumonectomy; nodules must be located away from central airways, absent chest‑wall or mediastinal invasion, no clinically significant pleural effusion, no extrapulmonary metastatic disease, and no radiographic progression of primary or pulmonary sites while on initial therapy. Prior treatment required: administration of standard systemic chemotherapy for newly diagnosed osteosarcoma comprising cisplatin combined with doxorubicin **or** an ifosfamide‑based regimen, delivered before enrollment (whether ongoing or completed). general_exclusion_criteria: Signed informed consent obtained from patient or parent/guardian; compliance with all applicable institutional, U.S. Food and Drug Administration, and National Cancer Institute regulations governing human subjects research.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Follicular lymphoma. Histology allowed: Grade 1, Grade 2, or Grade 3A follicular lymphoma, CD20 positive. Biomarkers required: CD20 positive (assessed by immunohistochemistry). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy exceeding prednisone 10 mg/day; patients with bulky cervical adenopathy threatening airway patency; patients with stage I follicular lymphoma; patients suitable for definitive radiation therapy with curative intent; recent major surgery or unresolved postoperative complications within four weeks; active chronic hepatitis B DNA polymerase chain reaction positive or active hepatitis C RNA positive infection; known HIV infection; active bacterial, viral, fungal, mycobacterial, parasitic, or other infection at enrollment; prior malignancy other than non‑melanoma skin cancer or adequately treated carcinoma in situ unless disease-free ≥2 years; receipt of live attenuated vaccinations within seven days preceding dosing; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, congestive heart failure NYHA class III/IV, left ventricular ejection fraction <45%, significant arrhythmias, or abnormal electrocardiogram findings; pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during study; prior solid-organ or allogenic stem cell transplantation; history of haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis; active autoimmune disorders such as myocarditis, pneumonitis, Myasthenia Gravis, polymyositis, autoimmune hepatitis, systemic lupus erythematosus, rheumatoid arthritis, inflammatory bowel disease, antiphospholipid syndrome, Granulomatosis with polyangiitis, Sjögren’s syndrome, Guillain‑Barré syndrome, multiple sclerosis, vasculitis, or glomerulonephritis (unless remotely resolved and approved); hypersensitivity to anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibodies or components of epcoritamab; administration of live vaccines within twenty‑eight days prior to first dose; central nervous system lymphoma; peripheral neuropathy greater than grade 2; concurrent severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 infection or inadequate recovery/testing parameters; baseline corrected QT interval >470 milliseconds.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma (residual invasive disease). Cancer burden allowed: Residual invasive disease in breast and/or lymph nodes after neoadjuvant chemotherapy (minimum 2 mm nodal involvement if no breast invasion); no distant metastatic disease; does not permit isolated ductal carcinoma in situ / lobular carcinoma in situ, nor pathological complete response (pT0N0i or pT0N1micro). Prior treatment required: Completed neoadjuvant chemotherapy preceding definitive breast surgery with clear margins (lumpectomy with whole‑breast irradiation or mastectomy) and axillary staging/surgery; documented circulation tumor DNA positivity (detectable tumor‑specific mutation(s)) by central assay; concomitant bone‑modifying agents permitted; prior neoadjuvant immune‑checkpoint inhibitor allowed. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior sacituzumab govitecan, irinotecan, or other topoisomerase I‑containing antibody‑drug conjugates for early‑stage disease; any adjuvant chemotherapy delivered after surgery; prior hypersensitivity reaction to atezolizumab, sacituzumab govitecan, or their excipients. Biomarkers required: HER2 negative defined as either in‑situ hybridisation ratio \< 2.0 **or** average HER2 copy number \< 4 signals/cell **or** immunohistochemistry 0 or 1+; estrogen receptor negative (**<10 %** stained cells) **and** progesterone receptor negative (**<10 %** stained cells); detectable circulating tumor DNA harboring tumor‑specific mutation(s) by centralized testing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis on baseline CT; active autoimmune disease of any kind except controlled hypothyroidism, celiac disease, mild psoriasis, eczema, lichen simplectic chronicus or vitiligo; cardiovascular disease including New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, myocardial infarction within six months, unstable angina, or serious uncontrolled arrhythmia; active infectious illnesses such as unresolved bacterial infection, active tuberculosis, or viral hepatitis/B​‑type/hepatitis C infection; HIV-positive serostatus; uncontrolled diabetes mellitus type I or II; grade ≥ 2 unmanaged hyperlipidaemia, hypertriglyceridaemia or hypercalcaemia; impaired hepatic function (cirrhosis) or biliary obstruction; coagulation abnormalities leading to active bleeding diatheses; congenital Long QT syndrome or screened QTc >\ 480 ms; significant gastrointestinal disorder impairing drug absorption (ulcerative colitis, Crohn’s disease, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhoea grade > 2, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resections); recent major surgical procedures within four weeks of planned study treatment; simultaneous participation in another investigational therapeutics trial; administration of systemic immunosuppressants within two weeks of study start (excluding brief low‑dose steroids for allergies, COPD, orthostatic hypotension, or adrenal insufficiency); receipt of a live, attenuated vaccination within four weeks of initiating study therapy; pregnancy or breastfeeding; inability to comply with mandatory contraception requirements.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Acute myeloid leukaemia. Histology allowed: Acute myeloid leukaemia. Cancer burden allowed: High‑risk for post‑transplant relapse; measurable residual disease positive by standardized flow cytometry pre‑transplant **or** TP53‑mutated disease (enrolled regardless of MRD status). Prior treatment required: Planned receipt of reduced‑intensity conditioning allogeneic haemopoietic stem cell transplant from an HLA‑matched related or haploidentical donor using post‑cyclophosphamide graft‑versus‑host disease prophylaxis; availability of a non‑mobilised donor apheresis product; ECOG performance status ≤2; adequate hepatic, renal and cardiac function as specified; negative pregnancy test (women) and effective contraception for both genders. Prior treatment excluded: Maintenance therapy targeting FLT3‑ITD, IDH or BCR‑ABL mutations initiated post‑transplant; use of sirolimus in graft‑versus‑host disease prophylaxis; prior allogeneic stem cell transplant occurring >7 days before proposed NK‑cell infusion; prior solid‑organ transplantation; documented allergy to cellular therapeutic products; systemic glucocorticoids >10 mg prednisone daily on day of NK infusion; donor‑specific anti‑human leukocyte antigen antibodies with mean fluorescent intensity >1000 absent a desensitisation protocol. Biomarkers required: Either TP53 mutation (any level) **or** MRD positivity by flow cytometry (when TP53 wild‑type). Biomarkers excluded: Detectable FLT3‑ITD, IDH or BCR‑ABL mutations when planned for targeted maintenance therapy; donor‑specific antibodies MFI >1000 without desensitisation.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pneumonitis; uncontrolled congestive heart failure, unstable angina, ventricular arrhythmias; left ventricular ejection fraction ≤40 %; serum creatinine >2.0 mg/dL; total bilirubin >1.5× ULN (unless Gilberts or benign hemolysis); transaminases >3× ULN; oxygen saturation <90 % on ambient air; active uncontrolled bacterial, viral, fungal or opportunistic infection; known HIV infection; active hepatitis B or C infection; current pregnancy or lactation; inability or unwillingness to comply with contraception requirements; serious psychiatric disorder limiting adherence; prior grade ≥2 autoimmune hemolytic anemia; prior exposure to investigational agents within prohibited windows (excluding timing considerations here); any condition deemed by investigators to substantially raise risk of toxicity from CIML NK cells or interleukin‑2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Myelodysplastic syndrome. Histology allowed: Myelodysplastic syndrome. Cancer burden allowed: High‑risk for post‑transplant relapse as defined by any of the following: TP53‑mutation; monosomy 7; ≥10 % blasts at time of transplant; therapy‑related disease; progression toward overt acute leukaemia. Requirement of measurable residual disease is waived for TP53‑mutated cases; otherwise disease must be evaluable per institution’s assessment. Prior treatment required: Same as Space 1 (standard reduced‑intensity conditioning allogeneic stem cell transplant with matching donor, PT‑Cy GVHD prophylaxis, functional organ thresholds, ECOG ≤2, contraception/pregnancy testing). Prior treatment excluded: Same as Space 1 (targeted FLT3‑ITD, IDH, BCR‑ABL maintenance; sirolimus GVHD prophylaxis; prior allogeneic SCT >7 d before NK infusion; solid‑organ transplant; allergies to cellular products; systemic steroids >10 mg prednisone eq. on infusion day; untreated donor‑specific antibodies MFI >1000). Biomarkers required: TP53 mutation permits enrolment irrespective of MRD; otherwise high‑risk cytogenetic abnormalities (monosomy 7) or elevated blast proportion constitute qualifying high‑risk feature. Biomarkers excluded: Same as Space 1 (FLT3‑ITD, IDH, BCR‑ABL mutations earmarked for targeted maintenance; donor‑specific antibodies MFI >1000 without desensitisation).
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pneumonitis; uncontrolled congestive heart failure, unstable angina, ventricular arrhythmias; left ventricular ejection fraction ≤40 %; serum creatinine >2.0 mg/dL; total bilirubin >1.5× ULN (unless Gilberts or benign hemolysis); transaminases >3× ULN; oxygen saturation <90 % on ambient air; active uncontrolled bacterial, viral, fungal or opportunistic infection; known HIV infection; active hepatitis B or C infection; current pregnancy or lactation; inability or unwillingness to comply with contraception requirements; serious psychiatric disorder limiting adherence; prior grade ≥2 autoimmune hemolytic anemia; prior exposure to investigational agents within prohibited windows (excluding timing considerations here); any condition deemed by investigators to substantially raise risk of toxicity from CIML NK cells or interleukin‑2.

Cancer burden allowed: Classified as high‑risk for post‑transplant relapse; blast percentage in bone marrow aspirate/biopsy must be <10 % at baseline; disease meets criteria for allogeneic transplant intent. Prior treatment required: Identical to Spaces 1 and 2 (RIC allogeneic stem cell transplant from HLA‑matched related or haploidentical donor with PT‑Cy GVHD prophylaxis, donor apheresis collection, ECOG ≤2, requisite organ function, contraception/pregnancy safeguards). Prior treatment excluded: Identical to Spaces 1 and 2 (maintenance targeted therapy against FLT3‑ITD, IDH, BCR‑ABL; sirolimus containing GVHD regimens; prior allogeneic SCT >7 days before NK infusion; solid‑organ transplant; hypersensitivity to cellular therapeutics; systemic steroids >10 mg prednisone equiv. on infusion day; unaddressed donor‑specific antibodies MFI >1000). Biomarkers required: None mandatory beyond diagnostic confirmation; however, absence of FLT3‑ITD, IDH, BCR‑ABL actionable lesions destined for post‑transplant targeted maintenance is implied. Biomarkers excluded: FLT3‑ITD, IDH, BCR‑ABL mutations when planned for targeted maintenance; donor‑specific antibodies MFI >1000 lacking desensitisation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Hormone receptor‑positive, HER2‑negative invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable locally advanced disease or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Progression on ≥1 line of endocrine therapy for unresectable locally advanced or metastatic disease, with at least one line combining a CDK4/6 inhibitor; must be a chemotherapy candidate. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior chemotherapy for unresectable locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer. Biomarkers required: Hormone receptor positive (estrogen receptor positive and/or progesterone receptor positive) and HER2 negative (central confirmation). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Presence of breast cancer amenable to curative‑intent treatment.
Early recurrence occurring fewer than 6 months after completion of adjuvant or neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Symptomatic advanced/metastatic visceral spread at risk of rapid, life‑threatening complication.
Active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years.
History of (non‑infectious) pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease requiring steroids, or current pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease.
Active infection requiring systemic therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=12 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: any solid malignant neoplasm except prostate cancer and ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: any. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced or metastatic disease with measurable lesions per RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: CCNE1 amplification or FBXW7 deleterious mutation or PPP2R1A deleterious mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Uncontrolled congestive heart failure or clinically significant cardiovascular disease.
Severe hepatic insufficiency (e.g., Child–Pugh B/C).
Significant renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min).
Active untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
HIV infection or chronic viral hepatitis B or C.
Pregnancy or currently nursing.
Inadequate performance status outside the allowable ranges (ECOG > 2 or Lansky ≤49%).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >18. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: metastatic colorectal cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease with ≤6 liver lesions each <3 cm and at least one extracranial measurable lesion per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: fluoro­pyrimidine‑based chemotherapy and oxaliplatin‑containing chemotherapy and irino­tecan‑containing chemotherapy and anti‑VEGF therapy; if tumor is RAS wild‑type then anti‑EGFR therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Guanylate Cyclase C positive by immunohistochemistry (assessment during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Active infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy or documented fever (>38.1°C) within 7 days prior to leukapheresis.  
Tumor locations posing imminent risk of gastrointestinal, urologic, or vascular perforation with study drug infusion.  
Pregnancy or current lactation.  
Uncontrolled concomitant serious medical disorders that could compromise participant safety or data integrity (e.g., symptomatic cardiovascular disease, unstable arrhythmias, NYHA class III-IV heart failure, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, clinically significant hepatic insufficiency, renal impairment with estimated GFR <30 mL/min).  
Untreated or progressive central nervous system metastases.  
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or active hepatitis B or C infection.  
Performance status worse than acceptable limits (generally Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score >2).

Prior treatment excluded: prior oncolytic therapy AND prior CTLA-4 Ig medication. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: active herpetic infection or prior severe HSV‑1 complication; history of organ graft rejection within twelve months; systemic infection requiring intravenous antibiotics or antivirals within sixty days before enrollment; chronic systemic antiviral use with anti‑herpes activity unless for graft preservation; ongoing or recent (within five years) clinically significant autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression beyond standard graft prophylaxis; active hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or HIV infection; detectable hepatitis B/C viral loads within three months of enrolment; recent (<three months) transplant‑related viral infections (BK virus, Epstein–Barr virus, Cytomegalovirus); necessity to increase baseline immunosuppressive regimen within sixty days of starting study treatment; known active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
