Cancer burden allowed: metastatic or unresectable locally advanced disease. Prior treatment required: No more than two prior systemic chemotherapy regimens; prior anticancer therapy permissible if residual toxicity ≤ grade 1. Prior treatment excluded: Prior receipt of antibody‑drug conjugates targeting epidermal growth factor receptor or HER3 or containing a topoisomerase I payload; more than two prior systemic chemotherapy regimens. Biomarkers required: EGFR exon 19 deletion **or** EGFR exon 21 L858R substitution **or** EGFR exon 20 S768I mutation **or** EGFR exon 21 L861Q mutation **or** EGFR exon 18 G719X mutation. Biomarkers excluded: Primary EGFR exon 20 insertion mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of symptomatic congestive heart failure grade ≥2 or NYHA class ≥2; significant coronary artery disease including recent myocardial infarction or unstable angina; prolonged corrected QT interval >470 ms, third-degree AV block or complete left bundle branch block; active autoimmune disorders requiring systemic therapy (e.g., systemic lupus erythematosus, moderate-to-severe psoriasis, rheumatoid arthritis, inflammatory bowel disease); other malignancies diagnosed within past five years except adequately treated basal cell carcinoma, cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma, or carcinoma in situ; inadequately controlled hypertension (SBP >150 mmHg or DBP >100 mmHg despite two agents); grade 3 or higher chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, interstitial lung disease, or prior radiation‑induced pneumonitis; unresolved acute venous or arterial thromboembolism within six months; active or untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (previously treated brain metastases allowable if stable ≥4 weeks and off steroids >20 mg prednisolone equivalents); hypersensitivity to monoclonal antibodies or any excipient of the investigational product; prior allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation; cumulative anthracycline exposure >360 mg/m²; grade 2 or greater electrolyte disturbances (e.g., hypokalaemia); known HIV infection, active hepatitis B or C infection, or active tuberculosis; ongoing infectious processes requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy; current pregnancy or lactation; inability to comply with protocol procedures.

Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after prior CNS‑directed therapy, with objective evidence of disease on magnetic resonance imaging or cerebrospinal fluid cytology (isolated ocular recurrence accepted). Prior treatment required: Documented receipt of prior CNS‑directed therapy (chemotherapy ± radiation). Prior treatment excluded: Use of any anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, or anti‑PD‑L2 antibody; use of agents targeting CTLA‑4, OX‑40, or CD137; prior progression on ibrutinib or any Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients unable to undergo brain MRI; grade > 2 intracranian hemorrhage; concurrent warfarin or vitamin K antagonist use within 7 days; recent (<3 months) arterial thromboembolism; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression (≈Prednisone > 10 mg/day) or treated within past 2 years; requirement for high‑dose systemic corticosteroids (dexamethasone > 4 mg/day for > 3 consecutive days) near enrolment; unresolved toxicities greater than grade 1 from prior therapy; major surgery ≤ 2 weeks ago; unrecovered radiation‑related toxicities or recent cranial irradiation ≤ 2 weeks; live vaccination within 30 days; second malignant neoplasm actively progressing or treated within 3 years (excluding certain cured skin carcinomas); grade ≥ 3 hypersensitivity to study drugs; history of non‑infectious pneumonitis; ongoing/uncontrolled systemic infection (≥ grade 2) or recent IV antimicrobial course ≤ 14 days; clinically significant cardiovascular disease (NYHA class III/IV, uncontrolled arrhythmia, heart failure > class II, unstable angina, MI < 6 mo); uncontrolled hypertension; HbA1c > 8 %; non‑healing wound, ulcer, or bone fracture; congenital or acquired bleeding disorder; inability to swallow tablets/gastrointestinal absorption problems; prohibited concomitant meds influencing CYP3A4/5 strongly; use of enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsants not stopped appropriately; known HIV infection; active hepatitis B (surface antigen+) or hepatitis C (RNA detectable); active tuberculosis; prior allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation; underlying immunodeficiency; pregnancy, lactation, or plans to conceive/paternal involvement within study window.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 28 days to <18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Any underlying disease treated with allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: History of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant for benign or malignant disease PLUS diagnosed high‑risk HSCT‑associated thrombotic microangiopathy (must meet BOTH platelet count <50,000/µL **or** reduction >50% from peak post‑transplant AND evidence of microangiopathic hemolysis [schistocytes present **or** LDH > upper limit of normal **or** haptoglobin < lower limit of normal]; additionally must exhibit AT LEAST ONE high‑risk feature such as persisting TMA >2 weeks after calcineurin/siroli­mus adjustment, urine protein/creatinine ratio >2 mg/mg, serum creatinine >1.5 times pre‑TMA baseline, biopsy‑confirmed gastrointestinal TMA, TMA‑related neurologic deficit, unexplained pericardial or pleural effusion, pulmonary hypertension, Grade III/IV graft‑versus‑host disease, or serum C5b‑9 >244 ng/mL). Prior treatment required: Reception of an allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Prior treatment excluded: Use of eculizumab, ravulizumab, or defibrotide (current use or within three months, unless documented therapeutic failure). Biomarkers required: Platelet count <50,000/µL **or** >50% decrement; microangiopathic hemolysis markers as above; optional elevated serum C5b‑9 >244 ng/mL supporting high‑risk classification. Biomarkers excluded: ADAMTS13 activity <10%; evidence of shiga toxin‑producing Escherichia coli hemolytic uremic syndrome.
general_exclusion_criteria: Severe uncontrolled systemic bacterial or fungal infection requiring antimicrobial therapy; severe uncontrolled viral infection; malignant hypertension (blood pressure >99th percentile + 5 mm Hg with bilateral retinal hemorrhage or cotton‑ wool spots); poor prognosis with expected survival <3 months unrelated to HSCT‑TMA; current pregnancy or lactation; receipt of any investigational drug or device within four weeks before enrollment; alanine transaminase or aspartate transaminase greater than five times the institutional upper limit of normal within 28 days; positive HIV antibody or PCR test; employment relationship or close familial connection to sponsor, CRO, investigators, or study personnel; known hypersensitivity to any component of the study drug; any medical condition deemed by the investigator to pose undue risk to the participant.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: chronic myelomonocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: CMML type 1 or type 2. Cancer burden allowed: refractory or resistant disease after standard therapy (failure to respond to ≥4 cycles of hypomethylating agents or premature discontinuation owing to toxicity or disease progression). Prior treatment required: receipt of a hypomethylating agent (e.g., azacitidine or decitabine) for at least four cycles, or hydroxyurea for myeloproliferative‐type CMML when applicable. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Presence of overlapping myelodysplastic/myproliferative neoplasm syndromes other than CMML; acute promyelocytic leukemia or isolated extramedullary disease; candidate deemed immediately suitable for allogeneic stem cell transplantation; current active use of nicotine-containing products (tobacco, nicotine patch, vape); prior bone marrow transplant within six months or ongoing post‑transplant immunosuppression within three months; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy (excluding prednisolone ≤10 mg/day, inhaled/topical/local steroids); clinically significant cardiovascular disease—including NYHA Class III–IV heart failure, uncontrolled angina, myocardial infarction or stroke within six months, uncontrolled hypertension, symptomatic arrhythmias; baseline QTcF >470 ms (with appropriate correction for bundle‑branch blocks); concurrent active malignant disease requiring therapy aside from specified low‑risk lesions (non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, CIN, organ‑confined prostate cancer without progression); active untreated bacterial, fungal, or viral infection; documented HIV infection; active or chronic hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection; any other severe, uncontrolled systemic illness, major psychiatric disorder, or condition compromising safety, consent ability, or protocol adherence.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: estrogen‐receptor–positive and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2‑negative invasive carcinoma (any subtype). Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced unresectable **or** metastatic disease (measurable per RECIST v1.1 or evaluable bone lesions). Prior treatment required: eligibility for fulvestrant *and* receipt and documented tolerance of at least one cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor (palbociclib or ribociclib or abemaciclib). Prior treatment excluded: treatment with AKT, phosphoinositide‑3‑kinase or mammalian target of rapamycin inhibitors; use of CDK4/6 inhibitors in the metastatic setting (unless administered in the adjuvant/neoadjuvant setting with ≥12‑month drug‑free interval). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of another primary malignancy except if cured with no activity ≥2 years ago and low recurrence risk.
Radiotherapy within 2 weeks prior to starting study treatment.
Major surgery or significant trauma within 4 weeks of first dose.
Persistent CTCAE grade > 1 toxicities from prior anti‑cancer therapies (excluding alopecia), unless deemed non‑exacerbating.
Unstable spinal cord compression, untreated brain/leptomeningeal metastasis, or steroid dependence within 4 weeks.
Significant cardiac abnormality including prolonged QTc (threshold varies by concurrent CDK4/6 inhibitor), serious arrhythmias, NYHA class II or greater heart failure, EF < 50%, recent major cardiovascular procedure (<6 months).
Clinically significant dysglycemia (type Ⅰ or Ⅱ diabetes requiring insulin, HbA1c ≥ 8.0%).  
Previous allogenic stem‑cell transplantation or solid‑organ transplantation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: estrogen‑receptor–positive and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2‑negative invasive carcinoma (any subtype). Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease (must have measurable lesion(s) per RECIST v1.1 or evaluable bone lesions). Prior treatment required: receipt of prior endocrine therapy (tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitor or oral selective estrogen receptor degrader) as single agent or in combination, with radiographic evidence of recurrence or progression while on, or within ≤ 12 months after, completing (neo)adjuvant endocrine therapy; eligibility for fulvestrant *and* receipt and documented tolerance of at least one cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor (palbociclib or ribociclib). Prior treatment excluded: treatment with AKT, phosphoinositide‑3‑kinase or mammalian target of rapamycin inhibitors; use of CDK4/6 inhibitors in the metastatic setting (except when given in the adjuvant setting with ≥12‑month drug‑free interval); more than one line of chemotherapy for metastatic disease. Biomarkers required: central circulating tumor DNA profiling for PIK3CA, AKT1, PTEN status performed at screening (testing optional for eligibility). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 1 to 21 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: hepatoblastoma. Histology allowed: hepatoblastoma. general_exclusion_criteria:
Allogeneic stem‑cell transplantation within 6 months of trial entry.
Administration of any monoclonal antibody for anticancer therapy within 4 weeks of trial entry.
Residual toxicity from prior therapy exceeding Grade 1.
Use of oral Bruton’s tyrosine kinase inhibitors, B‑cell lymphoma 2 inhibitors, or PI3K inhibitors within five half‑lifes of planned dosing.
Active viral, bacterial, or systemic fungal infection requiring treatment.
Human immunodeficiency virus positivity.
Active central nervous system lymphoma.
Pregnancy or lactation.
History of another malignant neoplasm diagnosed within 2 years (excluding appropriately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or prostate cancer managed with observation).
Uncontrolled concomitant illness such as New York Heart Association Class III–IV heart failure, myocardial infarction within the preceding 6 months, left ventricular ejection fraction < 40 %, life‑threatening arrhythmias, unstable angina, or symptomatic brain metastases.
Significant psychiatric disorder impairing compliance with trial procedures.
Known hypersensitivity to any ingredient of the investigational product.
Presence of preexisting human anti‑drug antibodies.
Active autoimmune disease, immune‑mediated peripheral neuropathy, or other central nervous system autoimmune condition.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥3 and <30 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Primary central nervous system non‑germinomatous germ cell tumor. Histology allowed: Endodermal sinus (yolk sac) tumor or embryonal carcinoma or choriocarcinoma or malignant/immature teratoma or mixed germ cell tumor containing any malignant nongerminomatous element. Cancer burden allowed: Localized disease restricted to the suprasellar and/or pineal region (including bifocal lesions) with no metastatic spread evident on magnetic resonance imaging or cerebrospinal fluid cytology. Prior treatment required: Diagnostic craniotomy/resection or needle/core biopsy of the lesion and/or peri‑diagnostic corticosteroid therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior tumor‑directed systemic chemotherapy, external beam radiotherapy, craniospinal irradiation, hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation, or experimental antitumor agent administered before enrolment. Biomarkers required: Alpha‑fetoprotein greater than 10 ng/mL or beta‑human chorionic gonadotropin greater than 100 mIU/mL in serum or cerebrospinal fluid (screening assay required). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients who are currently pregnant.
Patients who are nursing mothers intending to breast‑feed.
Reproductively capable individuals unwilling or unable to use adequate contraception throughout the study.
Individuals with a documented history of significant neurodevelopmental genetic syndromes (e.g., Down syndrome, Fragile X syndrome, Williams syndrome, severe intellectual disability); however, participants with neurofibromatosis type 1 are permitted.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma. Histology allowed: glioblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: intracranial disease after maximal surgical resection; tumor not predominately infratentorial. Prior treatment required: maximal surgical resection plus scheduled initiation of external beam radiation therapy 14–42 days post‑operative. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic anticancer therapy; any prior cranial radiotherapy; concomitant investigational agents; planned major surgery; planned use of Optune™. Biomarkers required: IDH1 R132H‐negative by immunohistochemistry **and** MGMT promoter unmethylated **and** provision of comprehensive tumour genomics (whole‑exome/sequencing, whole‑genome copy‑number analysis, or equivalent). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of a different malignant neoplasm unless disease‑free ≥2 years or meets predefined low‑risk exceptions. Clinically significant intratumoral or peri‑tumoral haemorrhage judged substantial by investigators. Congestive heart failure New York Heart Association Class ≥2, uncontrolled hypertension (BP ≥ 160/95 mmHg), recent myocardial infarction (<12 months), unstable angina, recent coronary event (<3 months), serious arrhythmias, prolonged corrected QT (>450 ms males, >470 ms females), complete left bundle branch block or bifascicular block. Poor glycaemic control (fasting plasma glucose ≥ 200 mg/dL or HbA1c ≥ 8%). Ongoing untreated infection, chronic viral hepatitis B or C, active HIV infection, chronic hepatic disease (cirrhosis), chronic renal insufficiency, chronic pancreatic disease, chronic respiratory disease, or severe psychiatric disorder impairing protocol adherence. Active diarrhoea ≥grade 2 despite optimal medical management. Pregnancy or lactation. Severe gastrointestinal disorders compromising oral drug absorption (e.g., inflammatory bowel disease flare, extensive resections, persistent emesis/vomiting/diarrhea). Hypersensitivity to components chemically or biologically related to study drugs. Recent use (within 7 days) of enzyme‑inducing antiseizure medicines, potent cytochrome P450 3A inhibitors or inducers, or vitamin/herbal products known to affect metabolism. Concurrent use of warfarin or other coumarin derivatives (unless switched to acceptable alternatives).

general_exclusion_criteria: Patients with life‑threatening immune‑related adverse events associated with prior anti‑PD‑(L)1 therapy, especially grade 4 events (except controlled endocrine disorders) or any event causing permanent cessation of checkpoint inhibition.; Patients with a personal history of ataxia telangiectasia.; Use of any other investigational agents concurrently.; Known hypersensitivity to components of tivusertib or avelumab or structurally similar substances.; Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses making participation unsafe.; Pregnancy or lactation (due to teratogenic risk of study drugs).; Inability to ingest oral medication or conditions interfering with GI absorption.; Requirement to continue proton‑pump inhibitors that cannot be stopped.; Residual toxicities greater than CTCAE grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (exceptions: alopecia, peripheral neuropathy up to grade 2, hormonereplacement‑dependent endocrine deficits permissible).; Concomitant use of strong CYP3A4/CYP1A2 inhibitors or inducers, MATE1/MATE2K substrate medicines that cannot be safely substituted.; Chronic systemic glucocorticoid use exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents.; Baseline corrected QT interval (QTcF) longer than 470 milliseconds.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: metastatic invasive breast carcinoma (de novo stage IV at diagnosis). Biomarkers excluded: HER2 IHC 1+ or 2+ without demonstrable HER2 amplification by ISH.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma multiforme. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent or progressive disease meeting Radiographic Assessment in Neuro‑Oncology criteria after completing first‑line radiation. Prior treatment required: first‑line cranial radiation completed ≥6 months before Cycle 1 Day 1 and Karnofsky Performance Scale score ≥60 and archived formalin‑fixed paraffin‑embedded tumor tissue available. Prior treatment excluded: NA. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe brain injury or stroke.
Inability to undergo sequential magnetic resonance imaging assessments.
Any seizure history unrelated to the tumor.
Concurrent use of strong cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme inhibitors or inducers shortly before study drug initiation.
Use of concurrently administered seizurogenic medications.
Past medical history of interstitial lung disease, drug‑induced interstitial lung disease, or radiation pneumonitis requiring corticosteroids.
Severe or uncontrolled coexisting conditions such as advanced chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Previous receipt of known pneumotoxic agents (e.g., busulfan, bleomycin) resulting in pulmonary toxicity.
Elevated serum creatine phosphokinase (>5× ULN on two occasions).
Cardiovascular disorders including myocardial infarction within six months, New York Heart Association class II–IV congestive heart failure, unstable angina, or uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias.
Active serious pulmonary infections, including untreated tuberculosis, chronic active hepatitis B, or hepatitis C (particularly noted for the Japan cohort).
Unresolved adverse events graded higher than NCI CTCAE version 4.03 grade 1 at enrollment; persistent grade 2 toxicities may preclude participation.
Prior exposure to ataxia telangiectasia mutated (ATM) inhibitor combined with radiation therapy.
Diagnosed diabetes mellitus (Type I, Type II, or steroid‑induced) when enrolling in the optional food‑effect substudy.
Concomitant systemic glucocorticoid therapy when participating in the optional food‑effect substud y.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: multiple myeloma. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease with measurable disease (M‑protein ≥0.5 g/dL, or urine M‑protein ≥200 mg/24 h, or serum free light‑chain difference >100 mg/L, or biopsy‑proved plasmacytoma, or imaging‑detectable lesions). Prior treatment required: exposure to a proteasome inhibitor **and** an immunomodulatory drug **and** an anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody; 1–4 prior lines of systemic therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Major concurrent illness or organ dysfunction (including plasma cell leukemia, Waldenström’s macroglobulinemia, POEMS syndrome, primary AL amyloidosis).
Complete spinal cord compression or central nervous system involvement.
Active or historical autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression within two years (exceptions apply for controlled thyroid disorders, Type 1 diabetes, vitiligo, psoriasis).
Receipt of a live, attenuated vaccine within four weeks preceding study drug administration.
Allogeneic solid organ or stem cell transplantation with chronic graft‑versus‑host disease requiring >20 mg/day prednisolone-equivalent.
Active infections requiring ongoing antimicrobial therapy.
Significant cardiovascular disease classified worse than NYHA class II.
Uncontrolled hypertension, significant arrhythmias, myocardial infarction within six months, or symptomatic congestive heart failure.
Renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 ml/min) or severe hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, transaminases >2.5× ULN).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Psychiatric or substance-use disorders impairing compliance.
Legal incapacity or inability to give informed consent.

Known active central nervous system infiltration or leptomeningeal disease. Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; women of childbearing potential unwilling/unable to use adequate contraception. Concurrent use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 inducers or inhibitors incompatible with venetoclax metabolism. Presence of active secondary malignant neoplasm requiring systemic therapy within the past two years (exceptions permitted for adequately treated basal/squamous cell carcinoma of skin, in‑situ cervical carcinoma, superficial bladder carcinoma not recently treated, localized hormone‑responsive prostate or breast cancer). Major surgical procedure within thirty days or minor surgery within seven days prior to randomization. Clinically significant coagulopathy or platelet disorder precluding safe administration of study drugs. Autoimmune hematologic disorders (autoimmune hemolytic anemia, immune thrombocytopenia) that are uncontrolled at baseline. inability to comply with study visits, medication schedule, or completion of PRO questionnaires in accepted languages.

Clinically significant cardiovascular disease including NYHA Class III‑IV heart failure  
Severe hepatic impairment (e.g., Child‑Pugh B or C)  
Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min)  
Untreated or symptomatic central nervous system metastases  
Active infectious diseases such as HIV, chronic hepatitis B or C  
Performance status worse than ECOG 1 (poor functional status)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Primary central nervous system lymphoma. Histology allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: Disease confined to the central nervous system (brain, cerebrospinal fluid, vitreous fluid, spine) with no evidence of disease outside the CNS. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior chemotherapy or radiation therapy directed to primary CNS lymphoma; any prior anti‑programmed death‑1 (PD‑1), anti‑programmed death ligand‑1 (PD‑L1), anti‑programmed death ligand‑2 (PD‑L2), anti‑cytotoxic T‑lymphocyte-associated protein 4 (CTLA‑4), ox‑40, or cd137 targeted therapy; prior hematopoietic stem cell transplantation aimed at treating lymphoma. Biomarkers required: HIV‑negative serology. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Participants unable to undergo magnetic resonance imaging.; Presence of isolated intraocular primary CNS lymphoma without accompanying brain or spinal disease.; Receipt of any other investigational agents concurrently.; Severe grade ≥ 3 hypersensitivity reaction to pembrolizumab, high‑dose methotrexate, temozolomide, rituximab, or their excipients.; Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression within the preceding 2 years (excluding physiological replacements).; Recent live vaccination administered within 30 days prior to first study dose.; Progressively growing concomitant malignant neoplasm or malignancy needing active treatment within the past 3 years (except adequately treated superficial skin carcinomas or carcinoma in situ).; Past or current non‑infectious pneumonitis necessitating steroids.; Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus with glycated haemoglobin greater than 8 %; Conditions impairing oral capsule ingestion or causing marked gastrointestinal malfunction (e.g., malabsorptive syndromes, extensive gastric/small‑bowel resections, complete intestinal blockage).; Current use of potent/moderate CYP3A4/5 inhibitors or inducers that cannot be safely stopped.; Use of enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsants without substitution to non‑enzyme inducing alternatives.; Chronic hepatitis B surface antigen positivity or detectable hepatitis C viral RNA.; Active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.; Immunodeficiencies or chronic systemic glucocorticoid therapy exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents.; Previous allogeneic stem cell transplantation.; Large pleural effusions, ascites, or generalized oedema.; Significant untreated psychiatric illness or substance misuse interfering with protocol compliance.; Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or sire a child from screening until 120 days after the last study dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Any solid tumor. Histology allowed: Solid tumor malignancy. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable disease or metastatic disease. general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pneumonitis or pulmonary fibrosis. Active symptomatic cardiovascular disease such as New York Heart Association Class III/IV heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within six months, or serious arrhythmia. Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin > 2× ULN or AST/ALT >5× ULN). Severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min). Untreated or progressive central nervous system metastasis. Ongoing systemic infections including untreated HIV, chronic hepatitis B or C, or tuberculosis. Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals. Inadequate bone marrow reserve (ANC <1500/mm³, platelets <100,000/mm³, hemoglobin <9 g/dL). Concurrent participation in another investigational therapeutic study. Conditions precluding safe administration or absorption of oral medications (e.g., gastrointestinal obstruction, malabsorptive disorders).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Cutaneous T-cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: Stage Ib or higher cutaneous T-cell lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or progressive disease after at least two prior systemic therapies; measurable disease per Revised International Working Group criteria. Prior treatment required: Minimum two prior systemic antitumour regimens. Prior treatment excluded: Duvelisib or ruxolitinib discontinued due to toxicity. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of significant concurrent medical illnesses or psychiatric disorders precluding safe participation. Current pregnancy or breastfeeding; inadequate contraception compliance. Allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant within six months or active graft-versus-host disease requiring immunosuppression. Ongoing use of systemic corticosteroids exceeding 20 mg prednisone daily or other immunosuppressive drugs. Significant chronic liver disease, veno‑occlusive disease, or active alcoholism. receipt of a live vaccine within six weeks before first dose. Major gastrointestinal surgical procedures impairing oral drug absorption (e.g., bariatric surgery, total gastrectomy). Untreated or uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection (detectable viral load or CD4<200 without ART). Active hepatitis B or C infection not adequately controlled per protocol specifications. Active cytomegalovirus infection requiring therapy. Inability or refusal to take recommended prophylactic antimicrobial agents (Pneumocystis jirovecii, HSV/HZV). Concomitant use of potent CYP3A4 inducer or inhibitor agents unable to discontinue appropriately. Tuberculosis treatment within the past two years. Concurrent therapy for another active primary malignancy (excluding non‑melanomatous skin carcinoma treated curatively). Documented central nervous system or leptomeningeal involvement by lymphoma causing neurologic deficits. Clinically unstable cardiovascular, respiratory, or metabolic conditions judged to place the patient at excessive risk.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive carcinoma (ductal or lobular) that is estrogen receptor positive and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative. Cancer burden allowed: Stage II‑III disease with no evidence of locally recurrent or distant metastatic disease; must have detectable disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow by central immunohistochemical assay. Prior treatment required: Completion of definitive surgery plus indicated postoperative chemotherapy and/or radiation; currently receiving adjuvant endocrine therapy (anastrozole, letrozole, exemestane, fulvestrant, or tamoxifen that has been switched to an aromatase inhibitor ≥21 days before study entry for hydroxychloroquine arms). Prior treatment excluded: Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial; concurrent use of tamoxifen with hydroxychloroquine‑containing arm (unless switched as noted). Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive and HER2 negative on primary tumor; presence of disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow by IHC. Biomarkers excluded: Estrogen receptor negative/progesterone receptor positive phenotype; HER2 positive tumors.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of another prior invasive breast cancer (except DCIS >5 years earlier).
Other prior invasive non‑breast malignancies requiring disease‑free interval <5 years.
Chronic high‑dose systemic corticosteroid use (>specified doses) or other immunosuppressive agents.
QTc prolongation >480 ms on electrocardiogram.
Uncontrolled serious cardiac conditions (unstable arrhythmia, recent acute coronary syndrome, PCI/stent <6 months, NYHA class III–IV heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension).
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease, DLCO <50 % predicted, or resting oxygen saturation ≤88 %.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus.
Active severe infection or untreated opportunistic infection.
Significant hepatic impairment (cirrhosis, chronic active/hepatitis B/C).
HIV infection requiring antiretroviral therapy (due to PK interaction concerns).
Severe gastrointestinal disorders affecting oral drug absorption (ulcerative colitis, uncontrolled N/V/D, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resections).
Bleeding diatheses or requirement for therapeutic anticoagulation.
Retinal pathology (retinitis pigmentosa, macular degeneration, retinal vascular occlusion) or known chloroquine‑related ocular toxicity.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of adequate contraception in persons of reproductive potential.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease  
• Congestive heart failure greater than NYHA Class II or left ventricular ejection fraction < 55%  
• Chronic kidney disease with creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min or serum creatinine > 1.5×ULN  
• Significant liver dysfunction (AST/ALT/ALK phosphatase ≥ 2.5×ULN or total bilirubin ≥ 2.0×ULN)  
• Uncontrolled hypertension  
• Recent (within 4 weeks) major surgery, traumatic injury, or invasive procedure  
• Current or past active infection including untreated hepatitis B/C, HIV, or tuberculosis  
• Ongoing high‐dose corticosteroid (> 4 mg/day dexamethasone equivalents) or other potent immunosuppressants  
• Autoimmune disease prone to flare with immune stimulation  
• Psychiatric illness compromising safe participation (major depression, psychosis, suicidality, etc.)  
• Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals  
• Concomitant malignancy unless disease‑free for ≥ 2 years  
• Live vaccinations within 4 weeks preceding enrollment or planned during study  
• Required use of prohibited medications (e.g., strong CYP inhibitors/inducers, anticoagulants such as P2Y12 blockers or vitamin K antagonists)  
• Poor performance status (Karnofsky < 70%)

Significant gastrointestinal disorders such as extensive gastric or small‑bowel resections, Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, or baseline grade 2 or higher diarrhea.

general_exclusion_criteria:

Known chronic obstructive pulmonary disease or prior interstitial lung disease.
Moderate or severe persistent asthma within the past five years or uncontrolled asthma of any severity.
Severe untreated cardiovascular disease.
Any preexisting immunodeficiency disorders.
Peripheral neuropathy grade 2 or higher.
Uncontrolled active infections such as HIV or viral hepatitis.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor (metastatic or unresectable). Histology allowed: Any solid malignancy. general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with a history of clinically significant pneumonitis or pulmonary fibrosis.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease—including symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, or life‑threatening arrhythmias—and NYHA functional classification worse than II (or Class IIIB/IV).
Baseline corrected QT interval (Fridericia) greater than 470 milliseconds.
Severe hepatic impairment exceeding threefold the institution’s upper limit of normal for AST/ALT (except when attributable solely to liver metastasis) or total bilirubin >1.5× upper limit of normal.
Renal impairment with glomerular filtration rate less than 60 ml/min/1.73 m².
Active, uncontrolled infectious diseases.
Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals.
Known hypersensitivity or severe allergic reaction to peposertib, tivusertib, or substances with similar structure.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non squamous. general_exclusion_criteria: History of spinal cord compression or active untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; symptomatic brain metastases not stabilized; receipt of thoracic radiotherapy less than 4 weeks before cycle 1 day 1 or cumulative lung dose >30 gy within 6 months; prior interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids or unresolved at screening; severe baseline pulmonary impairment due to comorbid lung diseases or prior total pneumonectomy; uncontrolled cardiovascular conditions including qtcf >470 ms, myocardial infarction within 6 months, unstable angina within 6 months, lvef <50 %, nyha class 2‑4 heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension (>180/>110 mmhg); clinically significant corneal disease; administration of live or live‑attenuated vaccines within 30 days preceding study drug; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy within the last 2 years; immunodeficiency or chronic systemic corticosteroids >10 mg prednisolone equivalents up to 7 days before dosing; uncontrolled hiv infection; active or uncontrolled hbv or hcv infection; ongoing serious infection necessitating intravenous antimicrobial therapy; prior allogeneic stem cell or solid organ transplantation; history of grade 3 or higher hypersensitivity reaction to dato dxd, pembrolizumab, or excipients.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: any prostate carcinoma (commonly adenocarcinoma). general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias (sustained ventricular tachycardia, second‑ or third‑degree atrioventricular block without pacing), familial long QT syndrome or torsades de pointes predisposition; untreated or unstable major medical disorders compromising safety; concurrent participation in another interventional oncology trial; unresolved acute infections; uncontrolled hypertension; symptomatic congestive heart failure; impaired hepatic or renal function precluding study procedures; active secondary malignant neoplasm posing threat to life or interfer­ing with evaluation (except adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer or superficial bladder cancer disease‑free >3 years); pregnancy or breastfeeding (irrelevant here but commonly excluded); unwillingness or inability to give informed consent.

Plasmapheresis performed within 7 days prior to enrollment.  
Uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.  
Active or inadequately controlled hepatitis B or C infection (except as permitted under strict virologic suppression criteria).  
Symptomatic amyloid neuropathy, active POEMS syndrome, or active plasma cell leukemia.  
Untreated or progressing central nervous system involvement (brain mets) despite stable disease after appropriate therapy.  
Clinically significant uncontrolled cardiac disorders—including recent (< 3 months) myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary interventions, congestive heart failure NYHA class III–IV, uncontrolled hypertension, clinically significant arrhythmias, second‑degree Mobitz type II or third‑degree AV block, QTcF > 480 ms, left ventricular ejection fraction < 30%, history of ventricular tachyarrhythmia, or symptomatic bradycardia HR < 50 bpm.

Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin >2× upper limit of normal or transaminases >5× ULN).
Active untreated or progressive central nervous system metastases (brain or leptomeningeal disease).
Ongoing clinically significant infection requiring antibiotics, HIV infection, active hepatitis B or C infection.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Use of potent systemic glucocorticoids (>10 mg/day prednisolone-equivalent) or other immunosuppressive medication for therapeutic purposes.
Any other serious comorbidity, laboratory abnormality, or psychosocial circumstance judged by the investigators to interfere with safe conduct of the study or interpretation of results.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: small cell lung carcinoma. Left ventricular ejection fraction <45%. Significant uncontrolled cardiovascular disease. Concurrent malignant disease (other than predefined exceptions). Severe uncontrolled medical illnesses posing undue safety risk. Requirement for supplemental oxygen to maintain saturations. Dementia or altered mental status affecting participation. Acute events incompletely recovered from within 6 months. Untreated HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C infection (unless meeting strict virologic control criteria). Allergies or hypersensitivities to study drug excipients (dimethyl sulfoxide) or concomitant agents (fludarabine, cyclophosphamide, tocilizumab). Persistent (>Grade 1) adverse effects from preceding anticancer therapy (aside from alopecia). Major surgical procedure within 4 weeks before leukapheresis or scheduled within 4 weeks post‑infusion. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or plans to conceive/paternal exposure within 12 months after infusion. Prior allogeneic stem cell or solid organ transplantation.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of allergic reaction to elranatamab.; Ongoing investigation with other experimental agents for AL amyloidosis.; Active concurrent malignant neoplasm (exceptions limited to adequately treated skin carcinomas, in‑situ cervical cancer, stage I solid tumors in remission >2 years, low‑risk prostate cancer Gleason <7 PSA <10 ng/mL, or other indolent/localized cancers).; Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to conceive during study and up to four months post‑treatment.; Inadequate contraception compliance for women of childbearing potential or males.; Unresolved toxicities greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (aside from alopecia).; Fulfilment of diagnostic criteria for active multiple myeloma (CRAB manifestations or involved/uninvolved FLC ratio >100 absent CRAB).; Clinically significant untreated autoimmune disorders.; Positive serology for Human Immunodeficiency Virus.; Significant hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh C) or biliary obstruction.; Renal insufficiency with creatinine clearance <30 mL/min.; Baseline New York Heart Association class IV heart failure or acute decompensated congestion.; Recent (<3 months) acute coronary syndrome, unstable ventricular arrhythmias, or symptomatic orthostatic hypotension causing syncope despite optimal therapy.; Left ventricular ejection fraction <30%; severe valvular stenosis (valve area <1.0 cm²) or critical congenital heart disease.; Documented sustained ventricular tachyarrhythmia or aborted sudden death without implanted pacing/device support.; Corrected QT interval >550 ms on baseline electrocardiography without paced rhythm.; Electrocardiographic signs of acute myocardial ischemia or uncorrected conduction disturbances outside predefined benign patterns.; Planned major surgical procedure requiring general anaesthesia within 4 weeks before/randomisation or anticipated during study.; Intent to proceed with autologous stem‑cell transplantation within six months of starting protocol therapy.; Lack of capacity to give informed consent or adherence concerns due to psychosocial reasons.

Prior treatment required: Prior receipt of ruxolitinib monotherapy satisfying relapse/refactory criteria (≥24 weeks discontinued for lack of spleen response or loss of response, **or** <24 weeks with documented disease progression or intolerance requiring ≥10 mg bid dosing). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Received prior B‑cell lymphoma 2 homologous 3 (BH3)‐mimetic, bromodomain and extraterminal (BET) inhibitor, phosphoinositide 3‑kinase or telomerase inhibitor; used more than one Janus Kinase 2 inhibitor; undergone prior stem cell transplantation; presently eligible for stem cell transplantation; taking medications that interfere with coagulation or platelet function (aside from low‑dose aspirin ≤100 mg daily or low‑molecular‑weight heparin); receiving concurrent anticancer therapy for an active malignancy or myelofibrosis—including chemotherapy, radiotherapy, hormone therapy—not limited to permissible best‑available therapy overlap.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: mantle cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: mantle cell lymphoma (World Health Organization 2016 classification). Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease after at least 2 prior systemic therapies including a bruton’s tyrosine kinase inhibitor, and patient is post–chimeric antigen receptor T cell therapy or is ineligible for CAR‑T therapy. Prior treatment required: minimum 2 prior systemic regimens inclusive of a BTK inhibitor; receipt of CAR‑T therapy or documented inability to receive CAR‑T therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of solid organ transplantation.
Active or recent (<6 months) cerebrovascular accident, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or congestive heart failure class II or higher, or serious cardiac arrhythmia requiring medication.
Clinically significant pericardial effusion or pleural effusion.
Ongoing peripheral neuropathy greater than grade 1.
Presence of a demyelinating disorder such as charcot-marie-tooth disease.
Any secondary malignant neoplasm unless treated curatively with no evidence of disease for at least 2 years.
Follicular lymphoma that has undergone transformation to a more aggressive lymphoma subtype.
Concurrent use of systemic corticosteroids exceeding 30 mg prednisolone equivalents daily.
Receipt of a live or live‑attenuated vaccination within 30 days preceding study drug administration.
Known active central nervous system lymphoma involvement.
Uncontrolled active infections requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection that is not adequately suppressed on antiretroviral therapy.
Active hepatitis B virus infection (unless meeting protocol-specified antiviral control conditions) or active hepatitis C virus infection.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable or metastatic disease with at least one measurable lesion per RECIST v1.1; stable brain metastases permitted. Prior treatment required: Up to five prior systemic regimens in the metastatic setting—including HER2‐directed therapy—and must have received trastuzumab deruxtecan. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: HER2‑positive determined by ASCO‑CAP guidelines in a CLIA‑certified laboratory. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of allergic reaction to any component of ARX788.; Prior interstitial lung disease, pneumonitis, or other clinically significant lung disease; Requirement for supplemental oxygen.; Active ocular infections or chronic corneal disorders.; History of congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, unstable atrial fibrillation, cardiac arrhythmia, or myocardial infarction within six months preceding enrolment.; Grade 3–4 peripheral neuropathy.; History of unstable central nervous system metastases.; Severe, uncontrolled systemic disease (e.g., clinically significant cardiovascular, pulmonary, or metabolic illnesses).; Uncontrolled intercurrent illness or infection, including active symptomatic COVID‑19.; Other conditions limiting study compliance or interfering with assessment.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: Multiple myeloma. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease with documented progression after latest antimyeloma regimen. Prior treatment required: Immunomodulatory drug **and** Proteasome inhibitor **and** Anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody (minimum three prior lines of therapy). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior GPRC5D‑directed therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Active or historic central nervous system involvement with multiple myeloma.
Active systemic fungal, bacterial, viral, or other infection despite appropriate anti‑infective treatment at the time of leukapheresis.
Other protocol‑defined inclusion/exclusion criteria applicable.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: greater than 28 days up to younger than 22 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: de novo acute myeloid leukemia according to World Health Organization classification, including presentation as myeloid sarcoma or evolution from primary myelodysplastic syndrome with ≥ 10% blasts. Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed disease without prior definitive antileukemic therapy (permitted up to one intrathecal injection and hydroxyurea or low‑dose cytarabine ≤200 mg/m² per day for ≤7 days). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior anthracycline or anthracenedone exposure; prior therapy beyond one intrathecal dose, hydroxyurea, or low‑dose cytarabine; treatment‑related acute myeloid leukemia; acute promyelocytic leukemia; chronic myeloid leukemia in blast crisis; juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia; Down syndrome‑associated acute myeloid leukemia; Fanconi anemia; Kostmann syndrome; Shwachman‑Diamond syndrome; other inherited bone‑marrow failure syndromes. Biomarkers required: presence of a clonal de novo acute myeloid leukemiallelic genetic alteration when morphological blasts are <20%, and measurable residual disease evaluation by flow cytometry after induction. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Uncontrolled systemic fungal, bacterial, or viral infection.
Significant concurrent medical condition that would jeopardize patient safety or protocol adherence.
Use of strong or moderate CYP3A inducers within three days before enrolment.
Use of strong or moderate CYP3A inhibitors within three days before enrollement.
Female participants of child‑bearing potential lacking a recent negative pregnancy test.
Inability or unwillingness to employ adequate contraception throughout the study and for six months thereafter.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: endometrium (uterine lining). Histology allowed: endometrial intraepithelial neoplasia or complex atypical hyperplasia or atypical hyperplasia. Cancer burden allowed: non‑invasive pre‑malignant lesion confined to the endometrium (no stromal/myoinvasion). Prior treatment required: candidate for definitive hysterectomy or willing to accept progestin‑releasing intrauterine device. Prior treatment excluded: hormonal contraceptives, menopausal hormone therapy, progestin‑containing intrauterine device, selective estrogen receptor modulators, aromatase inhibitors, metformin therapy, GLP‑1 or dual GLP‑1/GIP‑1 receptor agonists, investigational agents, dofetilide, ulipristal, carbonic anhydrase inhibitors, ranolazine, vandetanib, dolutegravir, cimetidine, sulfonylureas, meglitinides, alcohol use disorder. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Personal history of pulmonary embolism, thrombotic stroke, arterial thrombosis, or deep vein thrombosis.
Pregnancy.
Breast-feeding/lactation.
Inability to comprehend protocol or unwillingness to sign informed consent.
Severe uncontrolled intercurrent illness or psychiatric/social circumstances limiting adherence to study procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥65 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Metastatic breast cancer. Histology allowed: Infiltrating breast carcinoma (any histologic subtype). Cancer burden allowed: Evidence of distant metastatic disease (any location). Prior treatment required: First‐use of a cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor (palbociclib or ribociclib) in combination with an aromatase inhibitor (letrozole, anastrozole, or exemestane) **or** fulvestrant. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor administration for metastatic breast cancer or in the neo-/adjuvant setting within the past 12 months; prior use of the designated endocrine partner lasting more than 30 days before registration. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive or Progesterone receptor positive (hormone receptor positive) and Human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of intolerance or allergic reaction to study medications; uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses compromising protocol adherence; concomitant participation in other investigative therapeutic studies; rapid progression of central nervous system metastases; current use of medicines known to extend the QT interval; ongoing active treatment for another malignant disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced unresectable or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Received antibody‑drug conjugate therapy and/or cytotoxic chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: Radiopharmaceutical therapy. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative and somatostatin‑receptor PET positive (≥80 % of RECIST meas­urable lesions with SUVmax greater than liver SUVmean). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
* Persistent adverse events from prior therapy graded worse than CTCAE Grade 1 (except alopecia).
* Significant cardiovascular disease.
* Active, uncontrolled, or symptomatic central nervous system metastases, carcinomatous meningitis, spinal cord compression, or lepto‑meningeal disease.
* Hypersensitivity or allergy to actinium‑225, gallium‑68, copper‑64, octreotate, or any excipient of dotatate imaging agents.
* Concurrent or recent malignant neoplasm whose course or treatment could confound assessment of study safety or efficacy.
* Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals.

Prior treatment excluded: Receipt of PEEL-224 and progression on irinotecan plus temozolomide combination. Biomarkers required: Presence of a FET‑ETS family translocation (e.g., EWSR1::FLI1, EWSR1::ERG, EWSR1::ETV1, EWSR1::ETV4, EWSR1::FEV, FUS::FLI1, FUS::ERG) confirmed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe or unresolved pneumonia/pneumonitides. Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant arrhythmias. Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5×ULN or ALT >110 U/L). Severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <70 mL/min/1.73 m²). Active untreated central nervous system metastasis or worsening neurological deficit. Ongoing serious infection or immunodeficiency (including HIV or chronic viral hepatitis). Pregnancy or lactation. Known hypersensitivity to polyethylene glycol (PEG)-containing compounds, camptothecin derivatives, temozolomide, or vincristine. Recent receipt (within stipulated washout windows) of additional anti‑cancer agents, strong CYP1A2/CYP3A4 modulators, solid organ transplantation, allogeneic stem cell transplantation, or cellular therapies. Poor functional status (Karnofsky <50% or Lansky <50%). Psychiatric or social circumstances precluding adherence to study procedures.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of active acute promyelocytic leukemia; active central nervous system disease related to AML; participation in another investigational study concurrently; unresolved grade >1 toxicity from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia); contraindicated concomitant medications (strong CYP3A4 modulators, sitagliptin family drugs); untreated/uncontrolled infectious illnesses; active severe hepatic impairment (bilirubin > 1.5×ULN despite permissible exceptions); creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min; left ventricular ejection fraction ≤ 35 %; systolic BP < 100 mmHg or uncontrolled hypertension; New York Heart Association Class II or higher; uncontrolled intercurrent medical disorders (heart failure, unstable angina, serious arrhythmias, pulmonary embolism/pneumonia/sepsis); active hepatitis B or C infection without virologic suppression; current pregnancy or breastfeeding; inability to comply with contraceptive requirements.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable locally advanced disease with at least one meas­urable lesion. Prior treatment required: Taxane AND trastuzumab AND trastuzumab deruxtecan. Prior treatment excluded: Prior sacituzumab govitecan or any other Trop‑2‑directed antibody‑drug conjugate. Biomarkers required: HER2 positive (ASCO/CAP 2018 criteria). Biomarkers excluded: Homozygous UGT1A1 *28 genotype.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Hypersensitivity to trastuzumab, sacituzum​ab govitecan, or any excipient of these products.
Untreated, symptomatic brain metastases or brain metastases requiring chronic corticosteroid therapy.
Leptomeningeal disease.
Major surgery within two weeks prior to study treatment initiation.
Pregnant or nursing persons.
Active uncontrolled medical conditions—including unresolved acute infections, unstable cardiovascular disease, uncontrolled hypertension, significant respiratory insufficiency, impaired hepatic or renal function outside protocol limits, uncontrolled seizures, or severe psychiatric illness affecting adherence.
Presence of a second active malignancy unless disease‑free for three years or classified as low‑risk based on predefined criteria (e.g., cervica­l carcinoma in situ, superficial bladder cancer, non‑melanoma skin cancer).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Prior treatment with an oncolytic virus therapy.
Systemic infection requiring intravenous antibiotics.
Chronic use of systemic antivirals with anti‑herpetic activity (e.g., acyclovir, valganciclovir).
Active significant herpetic infection or prior severe complication of HSV‑1 infection.
Positive hepatitis B surface antigen or detectable hepatitis C viral RNA (unless successfully treated and now negative).
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection.
History of stage III or IV invasive head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.
Long‑term corticosteroid use (>4 weeks) at doses greater than prednisone 20 mg/day (or equivalent).
Personal history of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation or solid organ transplantation.
Presence of another active malignancy (exceptions: non‑melanoma skin cancer, low‑risk prostate adenocarcinoma on active surveillance, or other cancers in remission ≥2 years).
Significant bleeding episode within the preceding 6 months posing increased procedural hemorrhage risk.

Contraindication to mandated anticoagulation prophylaxis. general_exclusion_criteria:
Active hepatitis B infection (HBsAg positive) or active hepatitis C infection (positive HCV PCR).
Uncontrolled HIV infection (unless CD4 ≥250/mm³, suppressed viral load <400 copies/ml, and stable ART).
History of severe allergic reaction or anaphylaxis to monoclonal antibodies or related biologics.
Active central nervous system involvement by lymphoma (parenchymal or leptomeningeal).
Presence of Grade 2 or higher adverse event attributable to prior cancer therapy.
Documented progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy.
Significant untreated cardiac disease (e.g., NYHA class III-IV, recent MI, unstable arrhythmias) or severe pulmonary disease.
Recent major surgery (≤4 weeks) or radiotherapy (≤7 days) preceding study entry.
Concurrent active malignancy requiring therapy (except low‑risk skin cancers, cervical/breast carcinoma in situ, well‑treated localized prostate cancer).
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or unwillingness to use acceptable contraception per study REMS requirements.
Clinically significant ongoing infection (bacterial, viral, fungal, mycobacterial, parasitic) or recent intravenous antibiotic therapy requiring hospitalisation.
Severe hepatic impairment beyond stipulated laboratory limits.
Renal insufficiency with estimated glomerular filtration rate <30 ml/min.
Any condition judged by the investigators to place the participant at unacceptable risk or interfere with adherence/compliance.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Major surgery within 14 days prior to study drug initiation or incomplete recovery from operative complications.
* Current use of prohibited concomitant medications that cannot be stopped ≥7 days before dosing.
* Participation in another investigational therapeutic trial concurrently.
* Receipt of additional antitumour agents outside the prescribed study regimen (unless medically mandated, prompting withdrawal).
* Concomitant immunosuppressive agents (other than allowable corticosteroids) such as systemic cytochrome P450 modulators (cyclosporine, tacrolimus) deemed risky.
* Herbal supplements potentially interacting with study drug pharmacokinetics.
* Hypersensitivity to any ingredient/excipients of abemaciclib.
* Active systemic bacterial infection necessitating IV antibiotics, active fungal infection, or demonstrable viral infection (known HIV positivity, active Hepatitis B or C).
* Significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing drug absorption (ulcerative disease, uncontrolled N/V/D, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resections).
* Cardiovascular histories posing elevated risk: neurogenic syncopal episodes, pathologic ventricular arrhythmias (ventricular tachyarrhythmia/fibrillation), or prior sudden cardiac death/arrest.
* Uncontrolled/severe comorbidities judged unsafe by investigators (e.g., chronic pancreatitis, chronically active hepatitis).
* Inability to ingest oral tablets.
* Severe respiratory illness (interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis, resting hypoxia requiring supplemental O₂, Grade ≥2 dyspnea).
* Renal insufficiency with calculated CrCl < 30 mL/min.
* Baseline grade ≥2 diarrheal disease due to underlying colonopathy (Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, post‑gastrectomy/small‑bowel resection sequelae).
* Documented non‑adherence/compliance problems with medical regimens.
* Any secondary malignant neoplasm diagnosed within the past 2 years with current activity, except satisfactorily treated basal or squamous cell cutaneous carcinomas, non‑melanoma skin cancers, or surgically cured cervical cancer.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of malignant diseases other than appropriately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, or other solid tumours cured with no evidence of disease for ≥3 years.
Type 1 diabetes mellitus or inadequately controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus.
Active or untreated brain or leptomeningeal metastases; formerly treated CNS metastases only if off steroid support, seizure free, neurologically stable, and imaging stable ≥4 weeks.
Advanced symptomatic visceral involvement posing imminent threat to life.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disorders occurring within predefined windows (congestive heart failure NYHA class II or higher within 6 months, myocardial infarction within 12 months, serious arrhythmias or conduction defects within 12 months).
Uncontrolled arterial hypertension (systolic ≥160 mm Hg or diastolic ≥100 mm hg) despite medical management.
Long QT syndrome, familial sudden cardiac death predisposition, or baseline corrected QT interval >480 ms or uninterpretable ECG.
Known hypersensitivity to gedatolisib, fulvestrant, palbociclib, or excipients thereof.
Pregnancy or current lactation.
Concurrent enrolment in another interventional clinical trial.

general_exclusion_criteria:
– Inability to undergo magnetic resonance imaging of the brain.  
– Presence of active systemic disease or uncontrolled intercurrent illness.  
– Prior malignant neoplasm requiring active treatment, except adequately treated basal/squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, in‑situ cervical cancer, superficial bladder cancer, or other cancer disease‑free for ≥ 3 years.  
– Inability to safely ingest oral medications or existence of gastrointestinal conditions impairing drug absorption.  
– Current human immunodeficiency virus infection, past progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy, or any active significant infectious process (bacterial, viral, or fungal).  
– Known hypersensitivity or anaphylactic reaction to paxalisib or its constituents.  
– Requirement for concomitant strong cytochrome P450 3A4 inhibition or induction affecting paxalisib metabolism.  
– Significant pulmonary co‑morbidities such as interstitial lung disease, severe resting dyspnoea, or dependence on supplemental oxygen.  
– Clinically meaningful inflammatory bowel disease (Crohn’s disease or ulcerative colitis) or chronic condition producing baseline grade 2 or greater diarrhoea.  
– Type I diabetes mellitus, uncontrolled type II diabetes mellitus (HbA1c > 9 % or fasting glucose > 140 mg/dL on two measurements within 14 days), or poorly controlled diabetic regimens.  
– Uncontrolled arterial hypertension despite optimized pharmacologic therapy.  
– Positive hepatitis B core antibody with detectable HBV DNA, positive hepatitis B surface antigen, or positive hepatitis C RNA; individuals meeting these virologic criteria are excluded.  
– Pregnancy, current lactation, or inadequate contraceptive compliance for women of child‑bearing potential and partners of men with fertile capacity.  
– Participation concurrently in another therapeutic clinical investigation.

Interstitial lung disease or pulmonary fibrosis evident on baseline imaging. Uncontrolled pleural effusion, pericardial effusion, or ascites. Current severe gastrointestinal disorder compromising drug absorption (e.g., swallowing difficulty, chronic diarrhoea, bowel obstruction, total gastrectomy). Recent major (>6 weeks) or minor (<2 weeks) surgical procedure. Urine protein ≥2+ or ≥1 g/24 h. Active serious infection or documented viral hepatitis B/C or HIV infection. Cognitive or psychiatric condition limiting ability to give informed consent. Pregnant, breastfeeding, or unwillingness to use adequate contraception. Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers during dose‐escalation portion. History of disabling toxicity from Aurora kinase or VEGF/VEGFR pathway inhibitors.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18‑74 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: De novo AML excluding acute promyelocytic leukemia, core‑binding factor AML (t(8;21)) and inv(16) AML. Cancer burden allowed: Newly diagnosed, untreated aside from hydroxyurea (may have ≤5 days ATRA if APL initially suspected), may arise from antecedent hematologic disease without prior allogeneic transplant. Prior treatment required: Planned intensive 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine + daunorubicin) together with midostaurin; hydroxyurea permitted before registration; ≤5 days ATRA allowed if APL was briefly presumed. Prior treatment excluded: Prior chemotherapy, immunotherapy, or investigational agents (aside from hydroxyurea) and any prior exposure to a menin inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Presence of FLT3‑ITD and/or FLT3‑TKD mutation **AND** NPM1 mutation identified in bone marrow or peripheral blood; baseline left ventricular ejection fraction ≥ 50%; creatinine clearance ≥ 60 mL/min; AST ≤ 2.5 × ULN, ALT ≤ 2.5 × ULN, total bilirubin ≤ 1.5 × ULN. Biomarkers excluded: Lack of FLT3‑ITD/TKD or NPM1 mutation; active central nervous system AML involvement.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of acute promyelocytic leukemia, inversion 16 AML, or t(8;21) core‑binding factor AML. Known active central nervous system involvement with AML. Use of strong CYP3A4 inducers within seven days of study start. Concurrent strong CYP3A4 inhibitors (unless administered as approved antifungal azoles with dose adjustment). Prolonged corrected QT interval (>450 ms). Positive HIV test. Active hepatitis B or C infection (exception: undetectable viral load within three months or vaccinated/resolved status). Consumption of grapefruit, Seville orange, or star fruit within three days of dosing. Cardiovascular disease classified as New York Heart Association class II or higher. Significant underlying pulmonary, renal, neurological, psychiatric, endocrine, metabolic, immune, hepatic, or cardiovascular disorders deemed unsuitable by investigators. Chronic respiratory disease requiring continuous supplemental oxygen. Malabsorptive conditions preventing oral medication absorption. Uncontrolled systemic infection or other medically unstable conditions. History of other malignant neoplasms except adequately treated cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, ductal carcinoma in situ of breast, basal/squamous cell skin carcinomas, or solid tumors fully resected and disease‑free ≥ two years. Receipt of any chemotherapeutic, immunotherapeutic, or investigational agent within one month of enrollment. Prior exposure to a menin inhibitor.

Active untreated infections. History of solid organ transplantation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Ongoing treatment with certain anticancer agents.
* Prior treatment with a Farnesyl Transferase Inhibitor or HRAS inhibitor.
* Major surgery (except minor/local procedures) within 28 days before enrollment without full recovery.
* History of spinal cord compression, leptomeningeal disease, or clinically active central nervous system metastases.
* Residual unresolved toxicities (aside from alopecia) from prior therapy.
* Documented autoimmune or inflammatory disorders within the preceding five years (exceptions noted).
* Active, uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infections requiring systemic therapy.
* inability to swallow, severe gastrointestinal functional impairment, or gastrointestinal diseases compromising oral drug absorption.
* Significant cardiovascular abnormalities—including recent (<6 months) acute coronary syndrome, myocardial infarction, stroke, unstable angina, mean QTcF ≥ 470 ms, or New York Heart Association/Class II or worse congestive heart failure.
* Presence of another invasive malignancy diagnosed within the last two years.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of another malignancy that is currently progressing or requires active treatment, or a diagnosis of another malignancy within the past 2 years.; Persistent adverse effects from prior therapy greater than Grade 1 or not returned to baseline.; Active autoimmune disease necessitating systemic immunosuppressive corticosteroid therapy.; Untreated central nervous system or brain metastases, or CNS metastases that have demonstrated progression.; Previous organ transplantation, including allogeneic hematopoietic stem-cell transplantation.; Clinically significant or uncontrolled cardiovascular disease.; Active infection with hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus.; Ongoing systemic steroid usage exceeding 10 mg daily prednisone-equivalent dosage.; Current acute bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring systemic antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral medication.; Initiation of or modification to anticoagulation therapy within three months preceding the first dose of investigational product.; Known medically serious vasculitic disorders, arterial aneurysms, or a history of myocarditis.; Acute gastrointestinal complications such as bowel obstruction occurring within 60 days prior to enrollment.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of myocardial infarction or unstable angina within the preceding year; severe peripheral vascular disease; symptomatic arrhythmia despite treatment; documented immunodeficiency including HIV infection; chronic active hepatitis B or C infection; uncontrolled infectious processes; serious comorbidities deemed to markedly increase risk (e.g., severe cardiovascular disease); concomitant use of strong cytochrome P450 3A4 inductors shortly before study drug administration; prior irradiation of a solitary RECIST target lesion unless progression is proven; known myopathy; other malignancies diagnosed within the past three years except indolent lesions such as cured cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, non‑melanoma skin cancer, localized prostate cancer, non‑muscle invasive bladder cancer, ductal carcinoma in situ, or Stage I uterine cancer; currently pregnant or lactating individuals; inadequate contraception for persons of reproductive potential; situations necessitating urgent tumor debulking owing to imminent threat to critical anatomy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma of the breast. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable locally advanced or metastatic disease not amenable to curative resection; disease‐free interval ≥6 months if recurrence following adjuvant or neoadjuvant non‑hormonal systemic therapy. Prior treatment required: Completed first‑line induction therapy containing pertuzumab and trastuzumab (pharmacokinetic formulation Phesgo) combined with appropriate chemotherapy for HER2‑positive advanced breast cancer. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior use of a phosphatidylinositol‑3‑kinase (PI3K), AKT, or mechanistic target of rapamycin (mTOR) inhibitor. Biomarkers required: HER2‑positive tumour (confirmed by central testing) **and** PIK3CA‑mutated tumour (confirmed by central testing). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Prior systemic non‑hormonal anti‑cancer therapy for locally advanced or metastatic HER2‑positive breast cancer before induction therapy; disease progression within 6 months of any HER2‑targeted therapy; history or active inflammatory bowel disease; type 2 diabetes requiring ongoing systemic treatment or any history of type 1 diabetes; active hepatitis B virus infection; clinically significant and active liver disease—including severe liver impairment, viral or other hepatitis, current alcohol abuse, or cirrhosis; symptomatic active lung disease, including pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; any history of leptomeningeal disease or carcinomatous meningitis; serious infection requiring intravenous antibiotics within 7 days prior to day 1 of cycle 1; any concurrent ocular or intraocular condition that would require medical or surgical intervention during the study period to prevent or treat potential vision loss; active inflammatory or infectious conditions in either eye or history of idiopathic or autoimmune‑associated uveitis in either eye.

Concomitant use of strong or moderate cytochrome P450 inhibitors or inductors. Baseline corrected QT interval > 470 ms. Personal history of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia, or suspicious features. inability to ingest oral medication or gastrointestinal diseases impairing drug absorption. Uncontrolled comorbidities—including uncontrolled hypertension, active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant arrhythmia, or severe psychiatric/cognitive impairment affecting adherence. Past or current pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease or corresponding imaging abnormalities. Active or latent tuberculosis infection. Recent major surgical operation within twenty‐eight days preceding study commencement.
