clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Systemic light chain amyloidosis. Histology allowed: Immunoglobulin light‑chain (AL) amyloidosis verified by Congo red staining with green birefringeance plus detection of t(11;14) translocation by fluorescent in‑situ hybridisation on bone‑marrow specimen. Cancer burden allowed: Disease requiring further therapy after at least one prior line of anti‑plasma‑cell treatment; cardiac risk stage I, II or IIIa according to the 2013 European Modified Mayo clinic staging (both natriuretic peptide and troponin values meeting stage I‑IIIa limits); plasma cell infiltrates ≤60% in bone marrow, no osteolytic bone lesions nor end‑organ damage characteristic of overt multiple myeloma; measurable disease defined by serum or urinary monoclonal protein ≥500 mg/dL **or** serum free light chain ≥20 mg/L together with abnormal κ/λ ratio **or** differential free light chain ≥20 mg/L; estimated life expectancy ≥3 months; left ventricular ejection fraction ≥35%. Prior treatment required: Minimum of one completed line of anti‑myeloma/plasmacyte directed therapy (type unrestricted). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior exposure to ixazomib (MLN9708) or venetoclax. Biomarkers required: Presence of t(11;14) chromosomal translocation by FISH (mandatory). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Recent major surgery or radiation therapy (≤14 days) preceding enrolment.  
• Ongoing significant unresolved toxicities greater than grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia).  
• Concurrent participation in another investigational drug study or receipt of other experimental agents within 30 days.  
 • Central nervous system involvement by disease.  
 • Documented hypersensitivity to venetoclax, ixazomib, dexamethasone or structurally related substances.  
 • Requirement for concomitant strong or moderate cytochrome P450 3A inhibitors or inducers, or potent P‑glycoprotein modulators.  
 • Active uncontrolled medical conditions: acute severe infection, untreated hepatitis B or C, uncontrolled hypertension, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant arrhythmia, myocardial infarction within six months.  
 • Significant psychiatric disorders or social circumstances impairing protocol adherence.  
 • Pregnant women, lactating mothers, or individuals with a positive pregnancy test at screening; inadequate contraception for participants of child‑bearing potential.  
 • Gastrointestinal pathology or procedures compromising oral drug absorption (including dysphagia).  
 • Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥ 3, or grade 2 accompanied by painful findings on exam.  
 • New York Heart Association functional class III/IV or Mayo/Cardiac stage IIIB (NT‑ProBNP > 8500 pg/mL).  
 • Diarrhea graded ≥ 3.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced unresectable disease or distant metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Documentation of germline loss‑of‑function mutation in BRCA1 or BRCA2 or PALB2. Prior treatment excluded: Systemic antitumour therapy for locoregionally recurrent or metastatic disease (aside from endocrine therapy started ≤28 days before randomisation); disease progression ≤84 days after completion of neoadjuvant or adjuvant chemotherapy; disease progression ≤12 months after completion of adjuvant or neoadjuvant PARP inhibitor or platinum‑containing regimen; disease progression ≤12 months after completion of adjuvant CDK4/6 inhibitor; disease progression ≤12 months after receipt of an oral selective estrogen degrader (including camizestrant) in the adjuvant setting. Biomarkers required: Germline deleterious mutation in BRCA1 or BRCA2 or PALB2 detected in peripheral blood (assessed at screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukemia; any known predisposition to bleeding; persistent severe cytopenia; severe or uncontrolled systemic disease or active uncontrolled infection; refractory nausea/vomiting, chronic gastrointestinal disease, inability to swallow formulation, or prior extensive bowel resection; history of another primary malignant neoplasm; persistent toxicities CTCAE grade ≥ 2 attributable to prior anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia); spinal cord compression, brain metastases, carcinomatous meningitis, or leptomeningeal disease; active untreated hepatitis B or C infection; active untreated human immunodeficiency virus infection; active tuberculosis infection; clinically significant cardiac arrhythmia or cardiovascular disease; ongoing exogenous reproductive hormone therapy or non‑topical hormonal therapy for non‑oncologic indications; major surgical procedure or serious trauma within 4 weeks preceding first dose or expected need for major surgery during study; palliative radiotherapy involving a limited field delivered within 2 weeks or broad-field irradiation affecting >30 % of bone marrow within 4 weeks before first dose; concurrent systemic anticancer therapy; concomitant strong/moderate CYP3A4 inducer or inhibitor, sensitive CYP2B6 substrate, narrow‑therapeutic‑index CYP2C9/CYP2C19 substrate, QT‑prolonging drug with torsades de pointes risk, or systemic atropine usage.

general_exclusion_criteria: Known hypersensitivity or contraindication to REM‑422 or its excipients. Active severe infection (excluding mild UTI or resolved bacterial pharyngitis). Documented active human immunodeficiency virus infection. Currently active hepatitis B or hepatitis C viral infection. Primary immunodeficiency disorders. Required daily systemic glucocorticoid therapy ≥ 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent. Receipt of live vaccination within 6 weeks prior to first dose. Concomitant use of potent cytochrome P450 3A inhibitors or inducers. Recent use (within 7 days) of acid‑reducing agents such as proton‑pump inhibitors or H₂‑blockers. Pregnant persons, individuals attempting conception, or breastfeeding women. Presence of malabsorptive syndromes, extensive gastrointestinal resections, or conditions markedly impairing gut integrity. Administration of prohibited concomitant medicines within 1 week before dosing. Significant untreated cardiac disease. Clinically significant infections.

High cardiovascular risk factors.

Concurrent enrollment in another therapeutic interventional clinical study.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Current or ongoing antitumour therapy outside the study protocol.  
• Significant cardiovascular disease: recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction, unstable angina, congestive heart failure, cardiomyopathy, symptomatic pericarditis, CABG, left ventricular ejection fraction < 50 %; resting QTcF ≥ 450 ms (men) or ≥ 460 ms (women); long QT syndrome, familial sudden death syndromes, or drugs that markedly prolong QT interval that cannot be stopped.  
• Uncontrolled arterial hypertension (systolic ≥ 160 mm Hg or diastolic ≥ 100 mm Hg).  
• Hepatic impairment: AST or ALT > 3× upper limit of normal; total bilirubin > 1.5× ULN (except Gilberts’ variant where threshold is higher).  
• Renal insufficiency: creatinine clearance < 60 ml/min.  
• Pancreatic enzymology abnormality: serum lipase > 1.5× ULN deemed clinically significant.  
• Recent major surgery complication requiring unresolved medical issue.  
• Pregnancy or breastfeeding (implied by lack of reproductive safety data).  
• Known active infections such as HIV, chronic hepatitis B/C, or other serious infectious diseases.  
• Poor functional status (performance status not compatible with study procedures).

general_exclusion_criteria: Any evidence of acute or uncontrolled illness or history of allogeneic organ transplantation. Mixed small cell lung carcinoma and non‑small cell lung carcinoma histology. Major surgical procedure within 28 days preceding first dose or planned during study. Active or prior documented autoimmune or inflammatory disorders (incl. IBD, systemic lupus erythematosus, sarcoidosis, vasculitis, rheumatologic diseases requiring systemic immunosuppression, autoimmune pneumonitis, autoimmune myocarditis) except isolated vitiligo, alopecia, stable hypothyroidism, mild skin conditions, or diet‑controlled celiac disease. Medical contraindication to receiving platinum‑based doublet chemotherapy. History of another primary malignant tumour except cured tumours with no activity ≥2 years, adequately resected non‑melanoma skin cancer, or in‑situ disease. Persistent toxicities graded ≥2 attributable to prior anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia/vitlgo). Neuropathy greater than grade 2 (unless investigator deems appropriate). Irreversible toxicities unlikely to worsen with study drugs (e.g., permanent hearing loss) permissible. Unstable spinal cord compression. Significant cardiovascular disease (symptomatic CHF, unstable angina, serious arrhythmias, uncontrolled AFib, sustained VT). Concomitant receipt of other chemotherapy, investigational agents, biological or hormonal antitumour therapy. Prohibited radiation therapy (only definitive RT ≥6 months prior, palliative cranial RT if stable, or limited palliative bone RT allowable). Untreated or progressive central nervous system metastasis; leptomeningeal carcinomatosis. Positive testing for active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection. Active hepatitis B surface antigen positivity, active hepatitis C viremia, or dual HBV/HCV/HDV coinfection. Human immunodeficiency virus infection that is not well controlled. Use of systemic immunosuppressive medications (exceptions noted for localized/topical steroids, physiological glucocorticoid dosing ≤10 mg prednisolone daily, pre‑medication steroids, single-dose palliative steroids). Live attenuated vaccination within 30 days prior to first dose. Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial (observational studies exempt). Hypersensitivity to study drug components. Pregnancy, lactation, or plans to conceive; inadequate contraceptive measures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia (small lymphocytic lymphoma phenotype). Cancer burden allowed: treatment‑naïve disease with measurable peripheral blood disease (absolute lymphocyte count >10 ×10⁹/L or white blood cell count ≥20 ×10⁹/L), no indication for therapy per International Workshop on CLL 2018 criteria and judged unlikely to require therapy within the next 6 months, no lymph node larger than 5 cm, no Richter transformation, lymphocyte‑doubling time ≥6 months when WBC >30 ×10⁹/L, serum immunoglobulin ≥400 mg/dL and not receiving chronic intravenous immunoglobulin. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior CLL‑directed therapy (chemotherapy, Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor, phosphatidylinositol‑3‑kinase inhibitor, Bcl‑2 antagonist, anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody or other targeted agents), prior radiotherapy or hormonal therapy for CLL within the past 2 years, prior anti‑programmed death‑1, anti‑programmed death ligand‑1 or anti‑programmed death ligand‑2 therapy, previous allogeneic or autologous stem cell/BM transplant. Biomarkers required: IGHV unmutated (≤2 % difference from germline sequence) confirmed by sequencing. Biomarkers excluded: del(17p) identified in ≥10 % of CLL cells by fluorescent in‑situ hybridisation.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe allergic reaction to any vaccine therapy.  
Never received tetanus vaccine.  
Recent receipt (within 2 weeks) of a non‑oncology preventative vaccine.  
Active, clinically significant autoimmune disease (exceptions: vitiligo, type 1 diabetes, stable autoimmune hypothyroidism, psoriasis not requiring systemic therapy).  
Uncontrolled autoimmune cytopenia.  
Known chronic infections with HIV, hepatitis B virus, or hepatitis C virus.  
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.  
Participation in another investigational study concurrently.  
Requirement for chronic systemic corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive/immunomodulatory agents (unless tapered off ≥1 week before first vaccine).  
Significant cardiovascular disorders: symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmia.  
Uncontrolled intercurrent acute infection or other serious illness.  
Evidence of central nervous system involvement by CLL.  
Allergic reaction to poly‑ICLC or chemically/similary composed substances.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor (any primary site). Histology allowed: any malignant histologic subtype. Cancer burden allowed: advanced disease that has relapsed after or is refractory to all locally available standard therapies; patients may have distant metastases, including historically treated central nervous system metastases that are radiographically stable and off corticosteroids for ≥14 days. Prior treatment required: progression after or lack of response to standard locoregional or systemic therapy appropriate for the patient's tumor. Prior treatment excluded: previous autologous or allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant **or** previous solid organ transplant **or** prior exposure to any therapeutic agent directed against CD24. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of a second primary malignancy (unless cured with no evidence of disease for ≥5 years, with specific exemptions noted).
Active untreated central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as serious infections, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within past six months, severe pulmonary hypertension, or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease limiting oxygen saturation.
Significant laboratory abnormalities indicating impaired hepatic, renal, or bone marrow function outside protocol-specified limits.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection, active hepatitis B or C infection.
Performance status worse than ECOG 2.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant pulmonary fibrosis or recurrent pneumonitis.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (NYHA class III‑IV heart failure, unstable angina, recent MI within 6 months).
Severe hepatic insufficiency (bilirubin > 1.5× ULN or ALT > 5× ULN).
Renal impairment (estimated GFR < 40 ml/min/1.73 m²).
Active uncontrolled central nervous system hemorrhage or symptomatic brain edema.
Untreated or progressive opportunistic infection (HIV with detectable viral load, chronic HBV/HCV viremia).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding (already captured in trial‐specific criteria but reiterated as universal exclusion).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Known allergies, hypersensitivity, or intolerance to TNG260, pembrolizumab, or related excipients.
Uncontrolled intercurrent medical illness limiting protocol adherence.
Active infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Concurrent enrollment in or planning participation in another investigational study.
Impairment of gastrointestinal function or disease substantially altering oral drug absorption.
Presence of an additional active prior or concurrent malignancy.
Central nervous system metastases accompanied by progressively worsening neurologic signs or symptoms.
Active hepatic disease of any etiology.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: uterine serous carcinoma. Histology allowed: serous carcinoma (any uterine carcinoma containing a serous component, excluding pure carcinosarcoma). Cancer burden allowed: recurrent or persistent disease with at least one measurably sized lesion per RECIST 1.1 (includes advanced/metastatic disease). Prior treatment required: at least one prior platinum‐based chemotherapy regimen for advanced/metastatic uterine serous carcinoma (adjuvant platinum permitted if recurrence occurs within 12 months of completion); tumor must have documented MSI or MMR status, and if tumor is MSI‑high or MMR‑deficient the patient must have previously received a PD‑1 or PD‑L1 immune checkpoint inhibitor (or be judged not suitable for such therapy). Prior treatment excluded: any prior cell‑cycle checkpoint inhibitor directed against CHK1, WEE1, or ATR. Biomarkers required: determination of tumor microsatellite stability/MSI status or mismatch repair proficiency/status; conditional requirement of prior PD‑1/PD‑L1 inhibitor therapy when MSI‑high or MMR‑deficient. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active uncontrolled brain metastasis or symptomatic leptomeningeal disease; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (NYHA functional class III–IV); serious hepatic impairment (bilirubin > 1.5× upper limit of normal despite liver metastases or transaminases > 5× ULN with liver metastases); severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min); active uncontrolled infection including HIV or chronic hepatitis B/C; history of grade ≥2 pulmonary toxicity or pneumonitis; pregnancy or breastfeeding; lack of appropriate contraception; ECOG performance status > 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18+. Sex allowed: male and female. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor. Histology allowed: any solid tumor histology. Prior treatment required: progression after at least one prior standard anticancer therapy (any modality) or intolerance to standard therapies. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of major surgical procedure, open biopsy, or significant traumatic injury with poorly healed wound within six weeks preceding first dose.
Allogeneic solid-organ transplantation.
Concurrent participation in another investigational drug study.
Active or historical autoimmune or inflammatory disorders (e.g., inflammatory bowel disease, systemic lupus erythematosus, sarcoidosis, granulomatosis with polyangiitis, rheumatologic diseases) aside from permitted mild forms (vitiligo, alopecia, stable hypothyroidism, uncomplicated dermatologic conditions, dietary-controlled celiac disease).
Documented allergy or hypersensitivity to durvalumab, tremelimumab, or structurally related substances.
Uncontrolled concomitant illness—including untreated active infection (including tuberculosis), uncontrolled hypertension (>140/90 mm Hg), interstitial lung disease, severe chronic gastrointestinal disease producing persistent diarrhea, or unmanaged psychiatric/social circumstances compromising adherence.
Primary central nervous system neoplasm (other than benign meningioma), existing brain metastasis, leptomeningeal carcinomatosis, uncontrolled seizure disorder, or cerebrovascular accident/stroke occurring within twelve months.
Congenital or acquired primary immunodeficiency.
Significant cardiovascular disease (myocardial infarction or unstable angina within twelve months, NYHA class II or higher heart failure, symptomatic ventricular or rapid atrial arrhythmias needing pharmacologic control, substantial peripheral vascular disease).
Coagulation abnormalities, active bleeding diatheses, or thrombotic events within twelve months.
Recent or current pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease.
Pregnancy or lactation (requiring a negative pregnancy test for females of reproductive potential and effective contraceptive measures for sexually capable individuals).
Requirement for total parenteral nutrition containing lipid emulsions.

Ongoing systemic steroids ≥10 mg prednisolone daily or anticipated need for immunosuppression. Recent therapeutic antibiotic use within two weeks. Untreated or progressive CNS/br​ain metastases (treated lesions acceptable if off steroids and stable ≥14 days). Significant cardiac impairment or recent major cardiovascular event. Uncontrolled hyper­calcaemia (for Part D cohorts). Severe hypertension, NYHA class II or higher heart failure, recent myocardial infarction or stroke (<3 months). History of hemolytic anaemia/Evans syndrome ≤3 months. Grade ≥ 3 peripheral neuropathy. Current therapeutic dosing of vitamin K antagonists (warfarin or related coumarins). Pregnancy or breastfeeding; women of childbearing potential not using effective contraception.

general_exclusion_criteria: Spinal cord compression or history of leptomeningeal carcinomatosis.; Untreated or symptomatic brain metastases (unless treated, asymptomatic, stable, and off high-dose steroids for ≥4 weeks).; Active infections including tuberculosis, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus.; Current or past non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, or unresolved suspicion of ILD/pneumonitis.; Significant underlying pulmonary disease compromising respiratory reserve.; Cardiac abnormalities: clinically significant arrhythmias, uncontrolled hypertension, acute coronary syndromes or major interventions within 6 months, cerebrovascular accidents or TIA within 6 months, NYHA Class II or higher heart failure, existing cardiomyopathy, serious valve disease, mean QTcF > 470 ms, or any condition increasing risk of torsades de pointes.; Persistent severe cytopenia (>2 weeks) from any cause.; Predisposition to bleeding.; Personal history of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia, or findings suggesting evolving MDS/AML.; Recent (<3 months) arterial or venous thrombosis (when applicable).; Chronic gastrointestinal disorders preventing oral intake or malabsorption interfering with drug delivery.

Cancer burden allowed: Any. Prior treatment required: Participant must have been treated on a qualifying parent pembrolizumab study and be presently receiving pembrolizumab monotherapy, a pembrolizumab‑based combination regimen, or lenvatinib as assigned in the parent protocol. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Severe (Grade ≥3) hypersensitivity reaction to pembrolizumab or its excipients. Receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days prior to initiation of the Second Course Phase. Diagnosis of immunodeficiency or chronic systemic glucocorticoid use greater than 10 mg/day prednisolone equivalents—or any other immunosuppressive therapy—within 7 days of Cycle 1 Day 1 of the Second Course Phase. Presence of a concurrently progressing or actively treated additional malignant neoplasm (exceptions: cured carcinoma in situ, Stage I disease, non‑ulcerated thin melanomas, basal/squamous cell skin cancers, in‑situ cervical or breast cancer managed definitively). Known active central nervous system metastasis or leptomeningeal disease. Active autoimmune disorder necessitating systemic therapy within the past 2 years (physiologic hormone replacements exempt). History of non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids or existing pneumonitis. Interstitial lung disease in participants with non‑small cell lung cancer. Ongoing infectious process demanding systemic antimicrobial therapy. Documented Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection. Positive serology or medical history of Hepatitis B or Hepatitis C (unless permissible per parent study). Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or cause conception throughout the study period and until 120 days after the last dose. Significant cardiovascular pathology—including sustained arrhythmias, NYHA class III/IV congestive heart failure, or symptomatic coronary artery disease. Liver impairment meeting Child‑Pugh classification >B (scores >6). Uncontrolled thyroid disorders. Poorly regulated diabetes mellitus. Previous allogenic solid‑organ or bone marrow transplantation. Current active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection. 

Lenvatinib‑specific exclusions: Major surgical procedure undertaken within 3 weeks before first dose of study drug. Pre‑existent Grade ≥3 gastrointestinal or nongastrointestinal fistula. Protein excretion equal to or exceeding 1 gram per 24 hours. Left ventricular ejection fraction falling below locally accepted lower limit of normal measured by MUGA or echocardiography. Radiologically evident arterial encasement/invasion or intra‑tumour cavitation. Corrected QT interval (Fridericia formula) longer than 480 milliseconds. Clinically meaningful cardiovascular event occurring within 12 months of first dose (NYHA class III/IV heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction, cerebrovascular accident, or haemodynamically compromising arrhythmia). Conditions impairing oral absorption of lenvatinib (e.g., marked gastrointestinal malabsorption). Recent active hemoptysis (minimum half‑teaspoon bright red blood) within 3 weeks before initiating lenvatinib. Hypersensitivity to any constituent of lenvatinib.

Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or cause conception throughout the study period and until 120 days after the last dose. Significant cardiovascular pathology—including sustained arrhythmias, NYHA class III/IV congestive heart failure, or symptomatic coronary artery disease. Liver impairment meeting Child‑Pugh classification >B (scores >6). Uncontrolled thyroid disorders. Poorly regulated diabetes mellitus. Previous allogenic solid‑organ or bone marrow transplantation. Current active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection. 

Lenvatinib‑specific exclusions: Major surgical procedure undertaken within 3 weeks before first dose of study drug. Pre‑existent Grade ≥3 gastrointestinal or nongastrointestinal fistula. Protein excretion equal to or exceeding 1 gram per 24 hours. Left ventricular ejection fraction falling below locally accepted lower limit of normal measured by MUGA or echocardiography. Radiologically evident arterial encasement/invasion or intra‑tumour cavitation. Corrected QT interval (Fridericia formula) longer than 480 milliseconds. Clinically meaningful cardiovascular event occurring within 12 months of first dose (NYHA class III/IV heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction, cerebrovascular accident, or haemodynamically compromising arrhythmia). Conditions impairing oral absorption of lenvatinib (e.g., marked gastrointestinal malabsorption). Recent active hemoptysis (minimum half‑teaspoon bright red blood) within 3 weeks before initiating lenvatinib. Hypersensitivity to any constituent of lenvatinib.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Smoldering multiple myeloma.
Active systemic amyloid light-chain amyloidosis.
Active plasma cell leukemia.
Requirement for red blood cell or platelet transfusion or blood growth factor support within 14 days (arms 1‑11) or 7 days (arm 12).
Platelet transfusion or granulocyte colony-stimulating factor within 7 days or pegfilgrastim within 14 days prior to CBC evaluation.
Any tumor‑directed therapy (radiation, chemotherapy, immunotherapy) within 2 weeks of day 1 (radio‑immunotherapy within 6 weeks).
History of spinal cord compression with residual paralysis (dose escalation phase only).
Investigational anticancer therapy administered within 3 weeks of day 1.
Autologous stem cell transplantation performed < 1 month ago or allogenic SCT < 3 months ago.
Active graft‑versus‑host disease post‑allogeneic SCT.
Life expectancy < 3 months.
Major surgical procedure within 4 weeks preceding day 1.
Unstable cardiovascular disease (symptomatic myocardial ischemia, NYHA class III/IV congestive heart failure, significant arrhythmias, MI within 3 months, EF < 50%).  
Uncontrolled arterial hypertension.
Active severe infection necessitating intravenous antimicrobial therapy within 1 week.
Known active hepatitis A, B, or C infection.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection or seropositivity.
Severe gastrointestinal disorder precluding oral tablet ingestion or drug absorption.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Serious active psychiatric illness or other medical condition interfering with participation.
Hypersensitivity to any study medication components.
Peripheral neuropathy grade > 2 or painful grade ≥ 2 (specific to SVd, SPVd, SNd arms).
Concurrent strong/moderate CYP3A modulators or proton pump inhibitors within stipulated periods.
Recent use of strong or weak CYP3A4 inhibitors/inducers affecting PK run‑in assessments.
Positive Hepatitis B surface antigen with core antibody (+) irrespective of activity (for SKd arm considerations).
Allergic reaction/history of anaphylaxis to thalidomide, lenalidomide, pomalidomide, carfilzomib, dexamethasone, CELMoDs, or formulation excipients.
Refusal/unwillingness to abstain from blood donation per protocol specifications.
Unable or unwilling to comply with mandated thrombosis prophylaxis.
Presence of concurrent active malignant disease or prior malignancy within past 3 years (excluding appropriately treated superficial skin carcinomas, cervical/in‑situ breast carcinoma, or localized prostate cancer).

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, or current untreated/interrogatable ILD/pneumonitis.
Recent major surgery or radiation therapy within 4 weeks (palliative stereotactic radiation within 2 weeks).
Concurrent use of chronic systemic corticosteroids or other immunosuppressants (unless for AE management).
Concomitant use of potent CYP3A4 or P‑glycoprotein modulators, including certain PPIs.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease: myocardial infarction within 6 months, symptomatic congestive heart failure NYHA class II–IV, left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%, or QTc > 470 ms (female) / > 450 ms (male).
Active serious infections requiring intravenous antimicrobial therapy.
Untreated or unstable brain metastases/leptomeningeal disease causing clinical deterioration.
Significant ocular pathology affecting cornea.
Clinically significant fluid collections requiring intervention (pleural effusion, ascites, pericardial effusion draining within 2 weeks).
Spinal cord compression.
Substance abuse interfering with compliance.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Inadequate organ function outside predefined laboratory limits (renal, hepatic, hematologic) despite allowance noted elsewhere.

Clinically significant fluid collections requiring intervention (pleural effusion, ascites, pericardial effusion draining within 2 weeks).
Spinal cord compression.
Substance abuse interfering with compliance.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Inadequate organ function outside predefined laboratory limits (renal, hepatic, hematologic) despite allowance noted elsewhere.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic disease that is not amenable to surgery or definitive radiotherapy, with either measurable disease according to RECIST version 1.1 or bone‑only disease without measurable soft‑tissue lesions. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic anticancer therapy for advanced/metastatic disease; prior (neo)adjuvant endocrine therapy with recurrence during or within 12 months after the last dose of endocrine therapy; prior (neo)adjuvant cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor therapy with recurrence during or within 12 months after the last dose of CDK4/6 inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor‑positive and/or progesterone receptor‑positive and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2‑negative (tumor testing performed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of visceral crisis posing immediate threat to life; current or past central nervous system metastases; inadequate renal function; inadequate hepatic function; significant hematologic abnormalities; active infections such as HIV, hepatitis B or C; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including congestive heart failure; poor performance status (ECOG > 2).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Cutaneous melanoma. Histology allowed: Malignant melanoma of the skin. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable and/or metastatic disease with measurable lesion(s). Prior treatment required: Disease progression on anti-programmed death receptor‑1 monoclonal antibody therapy (administered as monotherapy or in combination). Prior treatment excluded: Significant hypersensitivity or intolerance to anti‑PD‑1 therapy that makes subsequent pembrolizumab unsuitable. Biomarkers required: Two copies of functional G protein‑coupled estrogen receptor (GPER) protein‐coding sequence detected. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of allogeneic solid organ or bone marrow transplant.
Uncontrolled active autoimmune disease or immune deficiency.
Concurrent medical conditions that would interfere with safe conduct of the study or interpretation of results.
Severe cardiac insufficiency, hepatic impairment, or renal dysfunction incompatible with study medication dosing.
Active untreated central nervous system metastasis or symptomatic leptomeningeal disease.
Known infection with human immunodeficiency virus, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: > 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Any. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Up to 1 prior hormonal regimen for advanced/metastatic disease (hormone‑CDK4/6 combinations permitted); up to 1 prior chemotherapy (including antibody–drug conjugates) for locally advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment excluded: Prior palazestrant; Prior phosphoinositide 3‑kinase inhibitor; Prior mammalian target of rapamycin inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive **and** HER2 negative (assessment performed on most recent archival metastatic tumour specimen). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of another malignant neoplasm whose course or treatment would interfere with evaluation.; Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease or cardiac repolarisation abnormalities.; Cerebrovascular accident or transient ischemic attack within six months before enrollment.; Pulmonary embolism or deep vein thrombosis within six months or heightened thrombotic risk.; Pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.; Leptomeningeal carcinomatosis or symptomatic spinal cord compression.; Gastrointestinal conditions impairing oral drug absorption.; Documented human immunodeficiency virus infection.; Significant hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh class B or C), active hepatitis B or C, chronic alcoholism, or established cirrhosis.; Severe hypersensitivity skin reactions (Stevens‑Johnson syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis, DRESS).; Ongoing acute or high‑risk infectious processes, including untreated opportunistic infections.; Psychiatric illness or other substantial medical/comorbidity compromising protocol compliance or data interpretation.
